1 """gate0.py -- GATE-0 validity harness for the content_extraction bake-off. 2 3 The anti-drb_72 gate. NO candidate score is trusted until ALL of these pass; any 4 failure => sys.exit(non-zero), no scores emitted. Four checks: 5 6 (1) SCORER-MATH canary -- gold body in -> F1 ~= 1.0 (>= 0.99); pure junk in -> 7 F1 ~= 0 (<= 0.05). Proves the scorer rewards perfect extraction and scores 8 chrome/SEO LOW (drb_72 was a HIGH score on garbage; this is load-bearing). 9 Uses the offline pure-Python ROUGE-N (no jieba, no network). 10 11 (2) PUBLISHED-NUMBER anchor -- CONDITIONAL: valid ONLY IF the OFFICIAL 12 WebMainBench scorer (eval_baselines.py) is located. If present, reproduce 13 the Dripper Table-2 per-extractor anchors within tolerance. If absent, the 14 check FLAGS that it fell back to blind re-derivation (it does NOT silently 15 pass and is NOT made circular by feeding candidate output as its own gold). 16 17 (3) PER-CANDIDATE LIVENESS -- each candidate runs on a KNOWN-GOOD page where 18 any working extractor returns substantial body. A stub / empty / load-fail / 19 import-error THERE FAILS LOUD (the dead-candidate discriminator). A low 20 ROUGE on a genuinely hard page is a REAL score and never trips this. 21 22 (4) FAITHFULNESS substring assertion -- every content-of-record candidate's 23 output spans must be verbatim substrings of the source visible text. An 24 extractive tool passes; a generative/paraphrasing path (ReaderLM-v2) is 25 flagged -- the structural never-crown demonstration. Harness-internal; 26 never touches strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance. 27 28 Lineage: a sha256 manifest (scorer config + tokenizer + anchor set + each 29 candidate id) is printed so a config mismatch surfaces here as a HARNESS BUG. 30 """ 31 32 from __future__ import annotations 33 34 import hashlib 35 import json 36 import os 37 import sys 38 from dataclasses import dataclass 39 40 from _candidates import Candidate, ExtractorLoadError, build_candidate_registry 41 from _scoring import ( 42 ANCHOR_TOLERANCE, 43 CANARY_JUNK_MAX_F1, 44 CANARY_PERFECT_MIN_F1, 45 OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N, 46 OFFICIAL_TOKENIZER, 47 PUBLISHED_ANCHORS, 48 SCORER_CANARY_ROUGE_N, 49 OfficialScorerStatus, 50 anchor_within_tolerance, 51 build_official_runner, 52 check_faithfulness, 53 locate_official_scorer, 54 load_webmainbench_pages, 55 rouge_n, 56 ) 57 58 59 class GateZeroError(RuntimeError): 60 """Raised fail-loud when a GATE-0 validity check fails (run is INVALID).""" 61 62 63 # A KNOWN-GOOD HTML page: any working extractor returns substantial body. Used 64 # by the liveness canary as the discriminator (dead-on-this != low-on-hard). 65 KNOWN_GOOD_HTML = ( 66 "Tirzepatide cardiovascular outcomes" 67 "" 68 "
Home About Contact Subscribe
" 69 "
Buy now! Limited offer cookies accept
" 70 "

Tirzepatide reduces major adverse cardiovascular events

" 71 "

In the SURMOUNT-MMO randomized controlled trial, tirzepatide reduced the " 72 "incidence of major adverse cardiovascular events compared with placebo over a " 73 "median follow-up of three years in adults with obesity.

" 74 "

The treatment group received a once-weekly subcutaneous dose escalated to " 75 "fifteen milligrams, and the absolute risk reduction was statistically significant " 76 "with a hazard ratio below one.

" 77 "
Copyright 2026 All rights reserved Privacy Policy
" 78 "" 79 ) 80 KNOWN_GOOD_GOLD = ( 81 "Tirzepatide reduces major adverse cardiovascular events. In the SURMOUNT-MMO " 82 "randomized controlled trial, tirzepatide reduced the incidence of major adverse " 83 "cardiovascular events compared with placebo over a median follow-up of three years " 84 "in adults with obesity. The treatment group received a once-weekly subcutaneous " 85 "dose escalated to fifteen milligrams, and the absolute risk reduction was " 86 "statistically significant with a hazard ratio below one." 87 ) 88 # Pure chrome/nav/ads/cookie-banner with NONE of the gold body (the drb_72 guard). 89 PURE_JUNK_TEXT = ( 90 "Home About Contact Subscribe Buy now Limited offer cookies accept " 91 "Copyright 2026 All rights reserved Privacy Policy Terms of Service Newsletter signup" 92 ) 93 94 # A genuinely-low extraction is NOT empty (real result on a hard page); the 95 # liveness canary must NOT trip on this. Used to prove the discriminator. 96 LOW_BUT_LIVE_OUTPUT = "follow-up of three years in adults with obesity." 97 98 # Minimum output length (chars) on the KNOWN-GOOD page below which a candidate is 99 # treated as DEAD/stub (env-overridable; the known-good gold is ~430 chars). 100 LIVENESS_MIN_GOOD_OUTPUT_CHARS = int( 101 os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_LIVENESS_MIN_CHARS", "60") 102 ) 103 104 105 @dataclass 106 class CanaryResult: 107 name: str 108 passed: bool 109 detail: str 110 111 112 def check_scorer_math() -> list[CanaryResult]: 113 """Check (1): gold-in -> ~1.0, junk-in -> ~0 with the offline ROUGE-N.""" 114 results: list[CanaryResult] = [] 115 116 perfect = rouge_n(KNOWN_GOOD_GOLD, KNOWN_GOOD_GOLD, n=SCORER_CANARY_ROUGE_N) 117 results.append( 118 CanaryResult( 119 "scorer_math_perfect", 120 perfect.f1 >= CANARY_PERFECT_MIN_F1, 121 f"gold-in F1={perfect.f1:.4f} (>= {CANARY_PERFECT_MIN_F1})", 122 ) 123 ) 124 125 junk = rouge_n(PURE_JUNK_TEXT, KNOWN_GOOD_GOLD, n=SCORER_CANARY_ROUGE_N) 126 results.append( 127 CanaryResult( 128 "scorer_math_junk", 129 junk.f1 <= CANARY_JUNK_MAX_F1, 130 f"junk-in F1={junk.f1:.4f} (<= {CANARY_JUNK_MAX_F1})", 131 ) 132 ) 133 134 # Inverse sanity: a genuinely-low-but-real extraction scores BETWEEN the two 135 # extremes -- it is neither perfect nor zero (proves the scorer is graded). 136 low = rouge_n(LOW_BUT_LIVE_OUTPUT, KNOWN_GOOD_GOLD, n=1) 137 results.append( 138 CanaryResult( 139 "scorer_math_graded", 140 0.0 < low.recall < 1.0, 141 f"low-but-real unigram recall={low.recall:.4f} (strictly between 0 and 1)", 142 ) 143 ) 144 return results 145 146 147 def check_published_anchor( 148 status: OfficialScorerStatus, candidates: list[Candidate], allow_gpu: bool 149 ) -> CanaryResult: 150 """Check (2): published-number reproduction -- COMPUTE-and-compare, never 151 green-on-nothing. 152 153 Three honest outcomes: 154 * DEFERRED (passing-but-flagged): the official scorer / benchmark file is 155 NOT located -> the published anchor cannot be reproduced here; the run 156 falls back to FLAGGED blind re-derivation (brief-sanctioned). Reported 157 with status, never a fake green. 158 * COMPUTED+PASS: official scorer located -> actually run the CPU extractors 159 (Trafilatura/Resiliparse/jusText/readability -- no GPU needed) on the 160 in-repo WebMainBench_100 split, score with the OFFICIAL scorer, and 161 assert each reproduces its Dripper Table-2 anchor +/- tolerance. 162 * COMPUTED+FAIL: an anchor miss => HARNESS BUG (wrong N / tokenizer / 163 variant / split) -> FAIL LOUD (the drb_72 wiring-bug class). 164 """ 165 if not status.available: 166 return CanaryResult( 167 "published_anchor:DEFERRED", 168 True, # brief sanctions the FLAGGED blind re-derivation fallback 169 "DEFERRED: official scorer/benchmark not located -> blind re-derivation " 170 f"fallback (FLAGGED, N={OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N}). reason={status.reason}", 171 ) 172 runner = build_official_runner(status) 173 pages = load_webmainbench_pages(status, limit=int(os.getenv("PG_CE_ANCHOR_PAGES", "100"))) 174 if runner is None or not pages: 175 return CanaryResult( 176 "published_anchor:DEFERRED", 177 True, 178 "DEFERRED: official scorer file present but its scorer API/jieba or the " 179 f"benchmark pages could not be loaded (pages={len(pages)}) -> FLAGGED re-derivation.", 180 ) 181 # Reproduce anchors on the CPU extractors only (the GPU MinerU anchor is 182 # validated on the run host). Compute mean official F1 per extractor. 183 from _scoring import average 184 185 cpu_by_key = {c.key: c for c in candidates if not c.needs_gpu and c.key in PUBLISHED_ANCHORS} 186 misses: list[str] = [] 187 checked: list[str] = [] 188 for key, cand in cpu_by_key.items(): 189 try: 190 scores = [runner(cand.extract(p["html"]), p["gold"]) for p in pages] 191 except Exception as exc: # noqa: BLE001 -- a dead extractor here is a real bug 192 misses.append(f"{key}: extractor/scorer failed ({exc!r})") 193 continue 194 mean_f1 = average(scores) 195 checked.append(f"{key}={mean_f1:.4f}(anchor {PUBLISHED_ANCHORS[key]:.4f})") 196 if not anchor_within_tolerance(key, mean_f1): 197 misses.append( 198 f"{key}: observed {mean_f1:.4f} vs anchor {PUBLISHED_ANCHORS[key]:.4f} " 199 f"(|delta|>{ANCHOR_TOLERANCE}) -> HARNESS BUG" 200 ) 201 if misses: 202 return CanaryResult("published_anchor:COMPUTED", False, "; ".join(misses)) 203 return CanaryResult( 204 "published_anchor:COMPUTED", 205 True, 206 f"reproduced {len(checked)} CPU anchors +/- {ANCHOR_TOLERANCE} " 207 f"(N={OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N}, {OFFICIAL_TOKENIZER}): {', '.join(checked)}", 208 ) 209 210 211 def check_candidate_liveness(candidate: Candidate, allow_gpu: bool) -> CanaryResult: 212 """Check (3): dead-candidate discriminator on the KNOWN-GOOD page. 213 214 A candidate that load-fails, imports-fails, or returns empty/stub on the 215 KNOWN-GOOD page FAILS LOUD. needs_gpu candidates are honestly SKIPPED (not 216 faked) when no GPU is available. 217 """ 218 if candidate.needs_gpu and not allow_gpu: 219 return CanaryResult( 220 f"liveness:{candidate.key}", 221 True, # honest skip, not a pass-with-fake-score 222 f"SKIPPED (needs_gpu, no GPU host): {candidate.impl_id}", 223 ) 224 try: 225 output = candidate.extract(KNOWN_GOOD_HTML) 226 except ExtractorLoadError as exc: 227 return CanaryResult( 228 f"liveness:{candidate.key}", False, f"DEAD (load/import failure): {exc}" 229 ) 230 except Exception as exc: # noqa: BLE001 -- any crash on the known-good page is dead 231 return CanaryResult( 232 f"liveness:{candidate.key}", False, f"DEAD (crashed on known-good): {exc!r}" 233 ) 234 n_chars = len((output or "").strip()) 235 if n_chars < LIVENESS_MIN_GOOD_OUTPUT_CHARS: 236 return CanaryResult( 237 f"liveness:{candidate.key}", 238 False, 239 f"DEAD/stub: returned {n_chars} chars on known-good page " 240 f"(< {LIVENESS_MIN_GOOD_OUTPUT_CHARS}); a working extractor returns the body.", 241 ) 242 return CanaryResult( 243 f"liveness:{candidate.key}", True, f"live: {n_chars} chars on known-good page" 244 ) 245 246 247 def check_candidate_faithfulness(candidate: Candidate, allow_gpu: bool) -> CanaryResult: 248 """Check (4): verbatim-substring faithfulness on the KNOWN-GOOD page. 249 250 Extractive content-of-record candidates MUST pass. The generative yardstick 251 is EXPECTED to fail and is reported as flagged-not-fatal (it never wins). 252 """ 253 if candidate.needs_gpu and not allow_gpu: 254 return CanaryResult( 255 f"faithfulness:{candidate.key}", True, "SKIPPED (needs_gpu, no GPU host)" 256 ) 257 try: 258 output = candidate.extract(KNOWN_GOOD_HTML) 259 except Exception as exc: # noqa: BLE001 -- liveness already reports load failures 260 return CanaryResult( 261 f"faithfulness:{candidate.key}", False, f"could not extract: {exc!r}" 262 ) 263 report = check_faithfulness(output, KNOWN_GOOD_HTML) 264 if candidate.extractive: 265 # Content-of-record: MUST be faithful (verbatim). 266 return CanaryResult( 267 f"faithfulness:{candidate.key}", 268 report.is_faithful, 269 f"verbatim_fraction={report.verbatim_fraction:.3f} " 270 f"({report.verbatim_spans}/{report.checked_spans} spans)" 271 + ("" if report.is_faithful else f" VIOLATION: {report.first_violation!r}"), 272 ) 273 # Generative yardstick: expected NOT faithful; flagged, never fatal. 274 return CanaryResult( 275 f"faithfulness:{candidate.key}", 276 True, # not fatal -- structural never-crown handles eligibility 277 f"YARDSTICK (generative): verbatim_fraction={report.verbatim_fraction:.3f} " 278 "-> structurally barred from content-of-record (never crowned).", 279 ) 280 281 282 def lineage_manifest(candidates: list[Candidate], status: OfficialScorerStatus) -> dict: 283 payload = { 284 "scorer_canary_rouge_n": SCORER_CANARY_ROUGE_N, 285 "official_rouge_n": OFFICIAL_ROUGE_N, 286 "official_tokenizer": OFFICIAL_TOKENIZER, 287 "anchor_tolerance": ANCHOR_TOLERANCE, 288 "published_anchors": PUBLISHED_ANCHORS, 289 "official_scorer_available": status.available, 290 "candidates": [ 291 {"key": c.key, "impl_id": c.impl_id, "role": c.role, "extractive": c.extractive} 292 for c in candidates 293 ], 294 } 295 blob = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True).encode("utf-8") 296 payload["lineage_sha256"] = hashlib.sha256(blob).hexdigest() 297 return payload 298 299 300 def run_gate0(*, allow_gpu: bool, candidates: list[Candidate] | None = None) -> dict: 301 """Run all four GATE-0 checks. Returns a report; caller decides exit code.""" 302 candidates = candidates if candidates is not None else build_candidate_registry() 303 status = locate_official_scorer(os.getenv("PG_WEBMAINBENCH_REPO") or None) 304 305 results: list[CanaryResult] = [] 306 results.extend(check_scorer_math()) 307 results.append(check_published_anchor(status, candidates, allow_gpu)) 308 for cand in candidates: 309 results.append(check_candidate_liveness(cand, allow_gpu)) 310 results.append(check_candidate_faithfulness(cand, allow_gpu)) 311 312 manifest = lineage_manifest(candidates, status) 313 all_passed = all(r.passed for r in results) 314 return { 315 "gate": "content_extraction_gate0", 316 "all_passed": all_passed, 317 "lineage": manifest, 318 "results": [{"name": r.name, "passed": r.passed, "detail": r.detail} for r in results], 319 } 320 321 322 def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int: 323 import argparse 324 325 parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="GATE-0 validity harness") 326 parser.add_argument( 327 "--allow-gpu", 328 action="store_true", 329 default=os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_ALLOW_GPU", "0") == "1", 330 help="run needs_gpu candidates (else honestly skipped, not faked)", 331 ) 332 args = parser.parse_args(argv) 333 334 report = run_gate0(allow_gpu=args.allow_gpu) 335 print(json.dumps(report, indent=2)) 336 if not report["all_passed"]: 337 failed = [r["name"] for r in report["results"] if not r["passed"]] 338 print(f"GATE-0 FAILED: {failed} -- NO scores trusted (anti-drb_72).", file=sys.stderr) 339 return 1 340 print("GATE-0 GREEN: scorer math + liveness + faithfulness all pass.", file=sys.stderr) 341 return 0 342 343 344 if __name__ == "__main__": 345 raise SystemExit(main()) 346

1	"""run_bakeoff.py -- score each extractor candidate, write ranked results.
2	
3	Loads each candidate by its EXACT pip/HF id, runs it on every labeled page in
4	the fixture (BYTE-IDENTICAL HTML per candidate -- isolation), scores with the
5	metric, and writes a ranked results JSON.
6	
7	Honest flags (LAW II):
8	  * needs_gpu candidates (MinerU-HTML, ReaderLM-v2) are gated behind --allow-gpu;
9	    without it they are registered-but-SKIPPED (status="skipped_needs_gpu"),
10	    NEVER given a faked score.
11	  * a candidate that load-fails records status="dead" with the error, NEVER a
12	    believable-low score (the drb_72 anti-pattern).
13	
14	Decision rule (brief §3): among candidates that do NOT regress recall below the
15	Trafilatura incumbent floor (minus tolerance, §-1.3 weight-not-filter), winner =
16	highest F1, ties broken toward the DETERMINISTIC/extractive extractor. The
17	generative yardstick (ReaderLM-v2) is STRUCTURALLY excluded from winner
18	selection by role -- a higher F1 can never crown it.
19	
20	GATE-0 must be GREEN before any score here is trusted: run_bakeoff refuses to
21	emit a winner if gate0 is red (anti-drb_72).
22	
23	Faithfulness engine is never touched -- this scores extractor OUTPUT vs gold.
24	"""
25	
26	from __future__ import annotations
27	
28	import argparse
29	import json
30	import os
31	import sys
32	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
33	
34	from _candidates import (
35	    Candidate,
36	    ExtractorLoadError,
37	    build_candidate_registry,
38	    is_eligible_to_win,
39	)
40	from _scoring import (
41	    OfficialScorerStatus,
42	    average,
43	    build_official_runner,
44	    check_faithfulness,
45	    locate_official_scorer,
46	    rouge_n,
47	    score_official_or_fallback,
48	)
49	from gate0 import run_gate0
50	
51	# §-1.3 weight-not-filter: a candidate may not regress recall below the
52	# incumbent floor minus this tolerance. Env-overridable, pre-registered.
53	RECALL_REGRESSION_TOLERANCE = float(os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_RECALL_TOL", "0.02"))
54	
55	# A candidate is win-eligible unless it has a SYSTEMATIC faithfulness failure
56	# (advisor blocker 2): a single per-page substring miss must NOT hard-drop the
57	# lead candidate. Below this fraction of faithful pages => systematic failure
58	# (a genuinely non-extractive path), which legitimately disqualifies it.
59	FAITHFULNESS_SYSTEMATIC_FLOOR = float(
60	    os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_FAITH_SYSTEMATIC_FLOOR", "0.80")
61	)
62	
63	
64	@dataclass
65	class PageScore:
66	    page_id: str
67	    f1: float  # PRIMARY official ROUGE-N F1 (or flagged re-derivation)
68	    scorer_used: str  # "official" | "fallback_rederived"
69	    recall: float  # decomposed completeness (re-derivation half; NaN under official)
70	    precision: float
71	    junk_fraction: float
72	    faithful: bool
73	    verbatim_fraction: float
74	
75	
76	@dataclass
77	class CandidateResult:
78	    name: str
79	    key: str
80	    impl_id: str
81	    license: str
82	    role: str
83	    eligible_to_win: bool
84	    status: str  # scored | skipped_needs_gpu | dead | needs_fixture
85	    detail: str = ""
86	    mean_f1: float = 0.0
87	    mean_recall: float = 0.0
88	    mean_precision: float = 0.0
89	    mean_junk_fraction: float = 0.0
90	    faithful_fraction: float = 0.0
91	    scorer_used: str = ""  # official | fallback_rederived (honest provenance)
92	    page_count: int = 0
93	    pages: list = field(default_factory=list)
94	
95	
96	def _load_clinical_fixture(fixture_path: str) -> list[dict]:
97	    """Load labeled clinical pages (label_status == 'labeled' only are scored)."""
98	    pages: list[dict] = []
99	    if not os.path.isfile(fixture_path):
100	        return pages
101	    with open(fixture_path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
102	        for line in fh:
103	            line = line.strip()
104	            if not line:
105	                continue
106	            rec = json.loads(line)
107	            if rec.get("label_status") == "labeled" and rec.get("gold_main_body_md"):
108	                pages.append(rec)
109	    return pages
110	
111	
112	def score_candidate_on_pages(
113	    candidate: Candidate,
114	    pages: list[dict],
115	    status: OfficialScorerStatus,
116	    official_runner=None,
117	) -> CandidateResult:
118	    """Score one candidate across all labeled pages; never fakes a dead score.
119	
120	    PRIMARY metric = the OFFICIAL WebMainBench ROUGE-N (N=5, jieba) via
121	    score_official_or_fallback when the cloned scorer is present; otherwise the
122	    FLAGGED re-derivation at N=5 (scorer_used recorded per page+result). The
123	    decomposed recall/precision halves come from the pure-Python pass and are
124	    used only for the §-1.3 recall non-regression floor + junk reporting.
125	    """
126	    base = CandidateResult(
127	        name=candidate.name,
128	        key=candidate.key,
129	        impl_id=candidate.impl_id,
130	        license=candidate.license,
131	        role=candidate.role,
132	        eligible_to_win=is_eligible_to_win(candidate),
133	        status="scored",
134	    )
135	    page_scores: list[PageScore] = []
136	    scorer_kinds: set[str] = set()
137	    for page in pages:
138	        html = page.get("raw_html", "")
139	        gold = page.get("gold_main_body_md", "")
140	        try:
141	            output = candidate.extract(html)
142	        except ExtractorLoadError as exc:
143	            base.status = "dead"
144	            base.detail = f"load/import failure: {exc}"
145	            return base  # FAIL LOUD -- no believable-low score
146	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
147	            base.status = "dead"
148	            base.detail = f"crashed: {exc!r}"
149	            return base
150	        primary = score_official_or_fallback(output, gold, status, official_runner=official_runner)
151	        scorer_kinds.add(primary.scorer_used)
152	        halves = rouge_n(output, gold)  # decomposed recall/precision/junk
153	        faith = check_faithfulness(output, html)
154	        page_scores.append(
155	            PageScore(
156	                page_id=page.get("page_id", ""),
157	                f1=primary.f1,
158	                scorer_used=primary.scorer_used,
159	                recall=halves.recall,
160	                precision=halves.precision,
161	                junk_fraction=halves.junk_fraction,
162	                faithful=faith.is_faithful,
163	                verbatim_fraction=faith.verbatim_fraction,
164	            )
165	        )
166	    base.page_count = len(page_scores)
167	    base.mean_f1 = average(p.f1 for p in page_scores)
168	    base.mean_recall = average(p.recall for p in page_scores)
169	    base.mean_precision = average(p.precision for p in page_scores)
170	    base.mean_junk_fraction = average(p.junk_fraction for p in page_scores)
171	    base.faithful_fraction = average(1.0 if p.faithful else 0.0 for p in page_scores)
172	    # If ANY page fell back, the candidate's headline F1 is honestly a mix; record
173	    # the strictest honest label (fallback if any page used it).
174	    base.scorer_used = "fallback_rederived" if "fallback_rederived" in scorer_kinds else "official"
175	    base.pages = [asdict(p) for p in page_scores]
176	    return base
177	
178	
179	def select_winner(
180	    results: list[CandidateResult], incumbent_key: str = "trafilatura"
181	) -> dict:
182	    """Apply the decision rule: recall non-regression floor + highest F1, ties
183	    to the deterministic extractor; generative yardstick excluded by role."""
184	    incumbent = next((r for r in results if r.key == incumbent_key and r.status == "scored"), None)
185	    recall_floor = (incumbent.mean_recall - RECALL_REGRESSION_TOLERANCE) if incumbent else 0.0
186	
187	    # Eligibility: extractive non-yardstick role (structural never-crown) AND no
188	    # SYSTEMATIC faithfulness failure. A single per-page substring miss must NOT
189	    # hard-drop the lead (advisor blocker 2 / §-1.3 no-new-hard-filter); only a
190	    # candidate that is non-extractive across many pages is disqualified.
191	    eligible = [
192	        r
193	        for r in results
194	        if r.eligible_to_win
195	        and r.status == "scored"
196	        and r.faithful_fraction >= FAITHFULNESS_SYSTEMATIC_FLOOR
197	    ]
198	    # §-1.3: drop anyone who regresses recall below the incumbent floor.
199	    passing = [r for r in eligible if r.mean_recall >= recall_floor]
200	    if not passing:
201	        return {
202	            "winner": None,
203	            "reason": "no eligible scored candidate cleared the recall non-regression floor",
204	            "recall_floor": recall_floor,
205	            "faithfulness_systematic_floor": FAITHFULNESS_SYSTEMATIC_FLOOR,
206	        }
207	    # Highest F1; tie-break already favors extractive (all eligible are extractive).
208	    winner = max(passing, key=lambda r: (r.mean_f1, r.mean_recall))
209	    return {
210	        "winner": winner.key,
211	        "winner_f1": winner.mean_f1,
212	        "winner_scorer_used": winner.scorer_used,
213	        "recall_floor": recall_floor,
214	        "faithfulness_systematic_floor": FAITHFULNESS_SYSTEMATIC_FLOOR,
215	        "incumbent": incumbent_key,
216	        "note": (
217	            "generative yardstick excluded by role (structural never-crown); "
218	            "no SYSTEMATIC faithfulness failure required (single-page miss never hard-drops); "
219	            "recall non-regression enforced (§-1.3); PRIMARY F1 = official scorer when present."
220	        ),
221	    }
222	
223	
224	def run(*, fixture_path: str, out_path: str, allow_gpu: bool, require_gate0: bool) -> dict:
225	    candidates = build_candidate_registry()
226	
227	    # GATE-0 must be green before any score is trusted (anti-drb_72).
228	    gate0_report = run_gate0(allow_gpu=allow_gpu, candidates=candidates)
229	    if require_gate0 and not gate0_report["all_passed"]:
230	        failed = [r["name"] for r in gate0_report["results"] if not r["passed"]]
231	        raise SystemExit(
232	            f"GATE-0 RED ({failed}) -- refusing to score (anti-drb_72). "
233	            "Fix the harness/candidates before trusting any number."
234	        )
235	
236	    pages = _load_clinical_fixture(fixture_path)
237	
238	    # PRIMARY scorer = official WebMainBench (N=5 jieba) when the cloned repo is
239	    # present; built once and injected. Absent -> FLAGGED re-derivation per result.
240	    status = locate_official_scorer(os.getenv("PG_WEBMAINBENCH_REPO") or None)
241	    official_runner = build_official_runner(status)
242	
243	    results: list[CandidateResult] = []
244	    for cand in candidates:
245	        if cand.needs_gpu and not allow_gpu:
246	            results.append(
247	                CandidateResult(
248	                    name=cand.name,
249	                    key=cand.key,
250	                    impl_id=cand.impl_id,
251	                    license=cand.license,
252	                    role=cand.role,
253	                    eligible_to_win=is_eligible_to_win(cand),
254	                    status="skipped_needs_gpu",
255	                    detail="registered but skipped (no GPU host); never faked.",
256	                )
257	            )
258	            continue
259	        if not pages:
260	            # No labeled gold yet -> honest needs_fixture, not a fake score.
261	            results.append(
262	                CandidateResult(
263	                    name=cand.name,
264	                    key=cand.key,
265	                    impl_id=cand.impl_id,
266	                    license=cand.license,
267	                    role=cand.role,
268	                    eligible_to_win=is_eligible_to_win(cand),
269	                    status="needs_fixture",
270	                    detail="no labeled clinical pages; clinical-axis score not trusted.",
271	                )
272	            )
273	            continue
274	        results.append(score_candidate_on_pages(cand, pages, status, official_runner))
275	
276	    scored = [r for r in results if r.status == "scored"]
277	    ranked = sorted(scored, key=lambda r: r.mean_f1, reverse=True)
278	    winner = select_winner(results) if scored else {"winner": None, "reason": "no scored candidates"}
279	
280	    report = {
281	        "layer": "content_extraction",
282	        "gate0_passed": gate0_report["all_passed"],
283	        "fixture_path": fixture_path,
284	        "labeled_page_count": len(pages),
285	        "primary_scorer": "official" if official_runner is not None else "fallback_rederived (FLAGGED)",
286	        "primary_scorer_reason": status.reason,
287	        "metric_components": {
288	            "general_primary": "WebMainBench OFFICIAL ROUGE-N F1 (N=5, jieba)",
289	            "general_fallback": "pure-Python ROUGE-N (N=5), FLAGGED when official absent",
290	            "clinical_secondary_teds": (
291	                "TEDS table-fidelity (clinical subset) -- needs_dep (apted/easyted) + "
292	                "needs_fixture (gold table trees); registered, honestly flagged not-yet-wired."
293	            ),
294	            "faithfulness": "verbatim-substring vs source visible text (harness-internal)",
295	        },
296	        "winner_selection": winner,
297	        "ranked_keys": [r.key for r in ranked],
298	        "results": [asdict(r) for r in results],
299	        "lineage_sha256": gate0_report["lineage"]["lineage_sha256"],
300	    }
301	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path) or ".", exist_ok=True)
302	    with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
303	        json.dump(report, fh, indent=2)
304	    return report
305	
306	
307	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
308	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Run content_extraction bake-off")
309	    parser.add_argument(
310	        "--fixture",
311	        default=os.getenv(
312	            "PG_CE_BAKEOFF_FIXTURE",
313	            "outputs/ret_bakeoff/content_extraction/clinical_gold_fixture.jsonl",
314	        ),
315	    )
316	    parser.add_argument(
317	        "--out",
318	        default=os.getenv(
319	            "PG_CE_BAKEOFF_RESULTS",
320	            "outputs/ret_bakeoff/content_extraction/results.json",
321	        ),
322	    )
323	    parser.add_argument(
324	        "--allow-gpu",
325	        action="store_true",
326	        default=os.getenv("PG_CE_BAKEOFF_ALLOW_GPU", "0") == "1",
327	    )
328	    parser.add_argument(
329	        "--no-require-gate0",
330	        action="store_true",
331	        help="(diagnostic only) do not abort on a red GATE-0; never use for a trusted run",
332	    )
333	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
334	
335	    report = run(
336	        fixture_path=args.fixture,
337	        out_path=args.out,
338	        allow_gpu=args.allow_gpu,
339	        require_gate0=not args.no_require_gate0,
340	    )
341	    print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))
342	    return 0
343	
344	
345	if __name__ == "__main__":
346	    raise SystemExit(main())
347

1 """smoke_test.py -- OFFLINE stub smoke for the content_extraction bake-off. 2 3 Proves, with NO GPU / NO network / MOCKED model loaders: 4 A. every file in this layer compiles (py_compile). 5 B. the SCORER-MATH canary passes (gold-in -> ~1.0, junk-in -> ~0, graded mid). 6 C. the per-candidate LIVENESS canary CORRECTLY FAILS on a simulated STUB 7 candidate that returns "" on the known-good page (exits non-zero) -- the 8 anti-drb_72 discrimination (a dead candidate must fail loud, not score low). 9 D. a genuinely-low-but-LIVE candidate (real body, partial) does NOT trip the 10 liveness canary (proves it discriminates dead from merely-poor). 11 E. the FAITHFULNESS substring check PASSES an extractive output and FAILS a 12 paraphrased/reordered output (the generative-rewrite discrimination). If it 13 can't fail the paraphrase the check is decorative. 14 F. needs_gpu candidates are honestly SKIPPED (not faked) with no GPU. 15 16 All real extractors (Trafilatura seam, resiliparse, justext, readability, vLLM, 17 mineru) are MOCKED here -- the smoke never imports the heavy deps. Exit 0 on 18 success, non-zero on any failure. 19 """ 20 21 from __future__ import annotations 22 23 import os 24 import py_compile 25 import sys 26 27 HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)) 28 sys.path.insert(0, HERE) # import sibling modules by bare name 29 30 import _candidates # noqa: E402 31 import gate0 # noqa: E402 32 from _candidates import Candidate # noqa: E402 33 from _scoring import ( # noqa: E402 34 OfficialScorerStatus, 35 check_faithfulness, 36 rouge_n, 37 score_official_or_fallback, 38 ) 39 40 41 def _fail(msg: str) -> None: 42 print(f"[SMOKE-FAIL] {msg}", file=sys.stderr) 43 raise SystemExit(1) 44 45 46 def _ok(msg: str) -> None: 47 print(f"[SMOKE-OK] {msg}") 48 49 50 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 51 # A. py_compile every file 52 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 53 54 def check_py_compile() -> None: 55 files = [ 56 "__init__.py", 57 "_scoring.py", 58 "_candidates.py", 59 "build_fixture.py", 60 "gate0.py", 61 "run_bakeoff.py", 62 "smoke_test.py", 63 ] 64 for fname in files: 65 path = os.path.join(HERE, fname) 66 try: 67 py_compile.compile(path, doraise=True) 68 except py_compile.PyCompileError as exc: 69 _fail(f"py_compile failed for {fname}: {exc}") 70 _ok(f"py_compile clean on {len(files)} files") 71 72 73 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 74 # B. scorer-math canary 75 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 76 77 def check_scorer_math() -> None: 78 results = gate0.check_scorer_math() 79 for r in results: 80 if not r.passed: 81 _fail(f"scorer-math canary '{r.name}' failed: {r.detail}") 82 _ok("scorer-math canary passes (gold->~1.0, junk->~0, graded mid)") 83 84 85 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 86 # Mocked candidate registry (NO heavy deps): stub / live / low / generative. 87 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 88 89 def _good_body_extractor(html: str) -> str: 90 """A working extractive tool: returns the article body verbatim.""" 91 # Pull the two sentences that ARE in the known-good HTML (extractive). 92 return ( 93 "Tirzepatide reduces major adverse cardiovascular events. In the SURMOUNT-MMO " 94 "randomized controlled trial, tirzepatide reduced the incidence of major adverse " 95 "cardiovascular events compared with placebo over a median follow-up of three years " 96 "in adults with obesity. The treatment group received a once-weekly subcutaneous " 97 "dose escalated to fifteen milligrams, and the absolute risk reduction was " 98 "statistically significant with a hazard ratio below one." 99 ) 100 101 102 def _stub_extractor(html: str) -> str: 103 """A DEAD/stub candidate: returns empty string on the known-good page.""" 104 return "" 105 106 107 def _low_but_live_extractor(html: str) -> str: 108 """A genuinely-poor-but-LIVE extractor: real partial body (verbatim), >min chars.""" 109 return ( 110 "tirzepatide reduced the incidence of major adverse cardiovascular events " 111 "compared with placebo over a median follow-up of three years in adults with obesity." 112 ) 113 114 115 def _generative_extractor(html: str) -> str: 116 """A generative yardstick: PARAPHRASES (not verbatim) -> faithfulness must flag.""" 117 return ( 118 "A weekly injection of the dual agonist drug lowered heart attack and stroke risk " 119 "versus dummy treatment across a three-year window among heavier patients, " 120 "with the benefit reaching statistical importance at the top dose level." 121 ) 122 123 124 def _make(name, key, extract, *, needs_gpu, extractive, role) -> Candidate: 125 return Candidate( 126 name=name, 127 key=key, 128 impl_id=f"mock:{key}", 129 license="mock", 130 role=role, 131 needs_gpu=needs_gpu, 132 extractive=extractive, 133 extract=extract, 134 ) 135 136 137 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 138 # C+D. liveness canary discriminates DEAD (stub) from LOW-BUT-LIVE 139 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 140 141 def check_liveness_discrimination() -> None: 142 live = _make("live", "live", _good_body_extractor, needs_gpu=False, extractive=True, role="baseline") 143 stub = _make("stub", "stub", _stub_extractor, needs_gpu=False, extractive=True, role="candidate") 144 low = _make("low", "low", _low_but_live_extractor, needs_gpu=False, extractive=True, role="candidate") 145 gpu = _make("gpu", "gpu", _good_body_extractor, needs_gpu=True, extractive=True, role="candidate") 146 147 live_res = gate0.check_candidate_liveness(live, allow_gpu=False) 148 if not live_res.passed: 149 _fail(f"liveness wrongly failed a LIVE candidate: {live_res.detail}") 150 151 stub_res = gate0.check_candidate_liveness(stub, allow_gpu=False) 152 if stub_res.passed: 153 _fail("liveness FAILED TO CATCH a stub candidate (returned '') -- anti-drb_72 broken") 154 _ok(f"liveness correctly FAILS stub candidate: {stub_res.detail}") 155 156 low_res = gate0.check_candidate_liveness(low, allow_gpu=False) 157 if not low_res.passed: 158 _fail(f"liveness wrongly tripped on a low-but-LIVE candidate: {low_res.detail}") 159 _ok("liveness correctly PASSES a genuinely-low-but-live candidate (dead != poor)") 160 161 # F. needs_gpu honestly skipped (not faked) when no GPU. 162 gpu_res = gate0.check_candidate_liveness(gpu, allow_gpu=False) 163 if not gpu_res.passed or "SKIPPED" not in gpu_res.detail: 164 _fail(f"needs_gpu candidate not honestly skipped: {gpu_res.detail}") 165 _ok("needs_gpu candidate honestly SKIPPED (not faked) with no GPU") 166 167 # Now prove the full gate0 EXITS NON-ZERO when a stub is in the registry. 168 registry = [live, stub] 169 report = gate0.run_gate0(allow_gpu=False, candidates=registry) 170 if report["all_passed"]: 171 _fail("gate0.run_gate0 reported all_passed with a stub candidate present") 172 # Confirm the would-be exit code is non-zero (mirror gate0.main's logic). 173 exit_code = 0 if report["all_passed"] else 1 174 if exit_code == 0: 175 _fail("gate0 with a stub candidate did not produce a non-zero exit") 176 _ok("gate0 with a stub candidate -> all_passed=False -> non-zero exit (FAIL LOUD)") 177 178 179 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 180 # E. faithfulness discriminates extractive (pass) from paraphrase (fail) 181 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 182 183 def check_faithfulness_discrimination() -> None: 184 html = gate0.KNOWN_GOOD_HTML 185 186 extractive_out = _good_body_extractor(html) 187 rep_ok = check_faithfulness(extractive_out, html) 188 if not rep_ok.is_faithful: 189 _fail( 190 f"faithfulness WRONGLY failed an extractive output " 191 f"(verbatim_fraction={rep_ok.verbatim_fraction:.3f}): {rep_ok.first_violation!r}" 192 ) 193 _ok(f"faithfulness PASSES extractive output (verbatim_fraction={rep_ok.verbatim_fraction:.3f})") 194 195 paraphrase_out = _generative_extractor(html) 196 rep_bad = check_faithfulness(paraphrase_out, html) 197 if rep_bad.is_faithful: 198 _fail( 199 "faithfulness FAILED TO FLAG a paraphrase -- the check is decorative " 200 f"(verbatim_fraction={rep_bad.verbatim_fraction:.3f})" 201 ) 202 _ok(f"faithfulness FLAGS paraphrase (verbatim_fraction={rep_bad.verbatim_fraction:.3f}) -> never-crown") 203 204 205 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 206 # Structural never-crown: a generative role cannot win even with higher F1. 207 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 208 209 def check_never_crown() -> None: 210 from _candidates import is_eligible_to_win 211 212 gen = _make("gen", "gen", _generative_extractor, needs_gpu=False, extractive=False, role="yardstick_non_sovereign") 213 det = _make("det", "det", _good_body_extractor, needs_gpu=False, extractive=True, role="baseline") 214 if is_eligible_to_win(gen): 215 _fail("generative yardstick is eligible to win -- never-crown is NOT structural") 216 if not is_eligible_to_win(det): 217 _fail("deterministic extractor wrongly marked ineligible") 218 _ok("structural never-crown holds (generative yardstick ineligible regardless of F1)") 219 220 221 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 222 # Sanity: rouge_n behaves (extra guard the scorer is not trivially constant). 223 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 224 225 def check_rouge_sanity() -> None: 226 same = rouge_n("alpha beta gamma delta", "alpha beta gamma delta", n=2) 227 if same.f1 < 0.99: 228 _fail(f"rouge_n self-overlap not ~1.0: {same.f1}") 229 disjoint = rouge_n("alpha beta gamma", "delta epsilon zeta", n=2) 230 if disjoint.f1 > 0.05: 231 _fail(f"rouge_n disjoint not ~0: {disjoint.f1}") 232 _ok("rouge_n sanity (self->~1.0, disjoint->~0)") 233 234 235 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 236 # G. faithfulness survives HTML entities + unicode (advisor blocker 2): 237 # a verbatim extractor whose DECODED output meets entity-encoded source must 238 # still pass -- else the >=floor win-gate would hard-drop the lead on artifact. 239 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 240 241 def check_entity_unicode_faithfulness() -> None: 242 # Source HTML carries entities (&   ') and an en-dash entity. 243 entity_html = ( 244 "

Dose & safety

" 245 "

The 5 mg dose reduced events by 30–40 percent in Smith's " 246 "randomized controlled trial of adults with type 2 diabetes and obesity.

" 247 "
" 248 ) 249 # A faithful extractor emits DECODED text (real "&", real space, en-dash, '). 250 decoded_extractive = ( 251 "Dose & safety. The 5 mg dose reduced events by 30–40 percent in " 252 "Smith's randomized controlled trial of adults with type 2 diabetes and obesity." 253 ) 254 rep = check_faithfulness(decoded_extractive, entity_html) 255 if not rep.is_faithful: 256 _fail( 257 "faithfulness WRONGLY failed a verbatim extractor over entity/unicode source " 258 f"(verbatim_fraction={rep.verbatim_fraction:.3f}); decode/normalize is broken: " 259 f"{rep.first_violation!r}" 260 ) 261 _ok( 262 "faithfulness survives HTML entities + unicode " 263 f"(verbatim_fraction={rep.verbatim_fraction:.3f}) -> lead not hard-dropped on artifact" 264 ) 265 266 267 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 268 # H. official-or-fallback PRIMARY scorer is honestly FLAGGED when absent 269 # (advisor blocker 1): no official scorer -> scorer_used must say fallback, 270 # never silently presented as the official published number. 271 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 272 273 def check_scorer_provenance() -> None: 274 absent = OfficialScorerStatus( 275 available=False, 276 eval_script_path="", 277 benchmark_jsonl_path="", 278 reason="smoke: no repo", 279 ) 280 scored = score_official_or_fallback("alpha beta gamma", "alpha beta gamma", absent) 281 if scored.scorer_used != "fallback_rederived": 282 _fail(f"absent official scorer did not flag fallback: {scored.scorer_used}") 283 # And when 'available' with an injected runner, scorer_used must say official. 284 present = OfficialScorerStatus( 285 available=True, 286 eval_script_path="x/eval_baselines.py", 287 benchmark_jsonl_path="x/benchmark/WebMainBench_100.jsonl", 288 reason="smoke: injected", 289 ) 290 scored2 = score_official_or_fallback( 291 "a b c", "a b c", present, official_runner=lambda c, g: 0.6402 292 ) 293 if scored2.scorer_used != "official" or abs(scored2.f1 - 0.6402) > 1e-9: 294 _fail(f"injected official runner not used as PRIMARY: {scored2}") 295 _ok("scorer provenance honest (absent->fallback_rederived FLAGGED; present->official PRIMARY)") 296 297 298 def main() -> int: 299 print("=== content_extraction bake-off OFFLINE smoke ===") 300 check_py_compile() 301 check_rouge_sanity() 302 check_scorer_math() 303 check_liveness_discrimination() 304 check_faithfulness_discrimination() 305 check_entity_unicode_faithfulness() 306 check_scorer_provenance() 307 check_never_crown() 308 print("=== SMOKE PASSED ===") 309 return 0 310 311 312 if __name__ == "__main__": 313 raise SystemExit(main()) 314

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-ret-002 (#1294) dedup layer — labeled near-dup PAIR fixture builder.
3	
4	LAYER 5 (near-dup collapse BEFORE basket weight; never drops a distinct claim).
5	
6	This builds the LABELED ground-truth PAIR fixture the dedup bake-off scores against. The
7	cardinal sin (per CLAUDE.md §-1.3) is a FALSE MERGE of two distinct independent sources — it
8	deletes a real corroborator from the basket. So the scored metric is pairwise collapse
9	precision/recall vs per-pair gold ``{syndicated_copy / distinct}`` with a PRE-REGISTERED
10	precision FLOOR = 0.97 (locked in run_bakeoff.py, not here, not post-hoc).
11	
12	ANTI-CIRCULARITY (the whole ballgame — advisor-flagged):
13	  The gold label MUST be independent of every candidate's own similarity. If we labelled
14	  ``syndicated_copy := MinHash >= 0.9`` the bake-off would be rigged in MinHash's favour. So
15	  positives come ONLY from CANONICAL IDENTITY signals that no dedup candidate sees:
16	    - same canonical body (normalized-body sha256) under a different source_url  -> EXACT positive
17	    - same source_url RE-FETCHED across different banked runs, body byte-identical -> EXACT positive
18	      (real provenance: the identical page captured twice = a known-same pair by construction)
19	    - same DOI / PMID under a different source_url                                -> EXACT positive
20	  Negatives come from cross-topic pairs (a drb_72 AI-labor row vs a drb_76 colorectal row is
21	  distinct by construction — different questions, different evidence universes).
22	
23	  The HARD bands decide the score but cannot be auto-labelled by objective rule:
24	    - edited-syndication near-dups (same url across runs, body DIFFERS by fetch jitter /
25	      truncation / boilerplate) -> emitted as a judge-PROPOSED ``pending_adjudication`` queue,
26	      NOT auto-scored. run_bakeoff.py FAILS LOUD if asked to score on unconfirmed labels.
27	    - same-topic similar-wording DISTINCT pairs -> the curated hard-negative SEED set
28	      (``fixtures/hard_negative_seeds.jsonl``), each pair carrying a written "why distinct"
29	      rationale (the human/two-family-authored part, clinical-safety-critical per the brief).
30	
31	  "Judge PROPOSES only, never sets the scored label" is enforced structurally: this builder
32	  never writes a scored label derived from any similarity score.
33	
34	SCALE (per design_must_fix.md #5 — "500 bodies exhaustive" was arithmetically impossible):
35	  exhaustive C(N,2) only when the labelled-identity body set is N<=80 (<=3,160 pairs); above
36	  that we STRATIFIED-SAMPLE pairs with a stated, seeded bound recorded in the manifest. Every
37	  sampling step is seeded; the seed is written to the fixture manifest.
38	
39	KEEP-ALL PROVENANCE: every pair retains BOTH members' stable source IDs (evidence_id + run +
40	snapshot) so the downstream conservation assertion (cluster/singleton member-ID union ==
41	input ID set) can prove no source was silently dropped.
42	
43	ISOLATION: this module is run from the REAL POLARIS checkout (where outputs/ snapshots live).
44	It locates the repo root via --repo-root / POLARIS_REPO_ROOT (default C:/POLARIS). It imports
45	NO heavy seam at module load; the offline smoke test mocks the loaders and uses synthetic data.
46	
47	Output (under ``fixtures/``):
48	  - ``dedup_pairs_fixture.jsonl``   : SCORED pairs (label in {syndicated_copy, distinct},
49	                                       label_source in {canonical_body, canonical_url_refetch,
50	                                       canonical_doi, cross_topic, curated_hard_negative}).
51	  - ``pending_adjudication.jsonl``  : judge-PROPOSED near-dup band, NOT scored.
52	  - ``dedup_member_bodies.json``    : member_id -> normalized body (scorer side-index).
53	  - ``dedup_fixture_manifest.json`` : counts, seed, sampling bound, snapshot provenance, flags.
54	  - ``hard_negative_seeds.jsonl``   : authored curated distinct-but-similar seed pairs.
55	
56	Run (offline, no GPU, no network — reads only banked snapshots):
57	  python scripts/dr_benchmark/retrieval_bakeoff/dedup/build_fixture.py \
58	      --repo-root C:/POLARIS --out-dir <this_dir>/fixtures
59	"""
60	from __future__ import annotations
61	
62	import argparse
63	import glob
64	import hashlib
65	import itertools
66	import json
67	import os
68	import random
69	import re
70	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
71	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
72	
73	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
74	# Constants (no magic numbers buried in logic; LAW VI).
75	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
76	
77	# Banked snapshot slugs. The brief says "6 banked snapshots"; the 5 below are the canonical
78	# extracted snapshots present under outputs/corpus_backups/extracted/. drb_72 additionally
79	# appears across many run dirs (the cross-run re-fetch positive pool). We pool the canonical
80	# 5 + every run-dir copy of any slug we find. If a snapshot is missing we FLAG it (and fail
81	# loud on a zero pool, never silently proceed on an empty fixture).
82	CANONICAL_SLUGS: Tuple[str, ...] = (
83	    "drb_72_ai_labor",
84	    "drb_75_metal_ions_cvd",
85	    "drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc",
86	    "drb_78_parkinsons_dbs",
87	    "drb_90_adas_liability",
88	)
89	
90	# Minimum normalized-body length to be eligible as a fixture member. Very short bodies
91	# (captcha stubs, nav chrome) are non-discriminative; excluded to keep the pair task honest.
92	MIN_BODY_CHARS: int = 120
93	
94	# Exhaustive C(N,2) only up to this body count; above -> stratified sampling.
95	EXHAUSTIVE_MAX_BODIES: int = 80
96	
97	# Stratified target counts (stated bound, recorded in the manifest).
98	TARGET_EXACT_POSITIVES: int = 400  # canonical-identity syndicated copies
99	TARGET_CROSS_TOPIC_NEGATIVES: int = 1200  # disjoint-topic distinct pairs
100	TARGET_PENDING_NEAR_DUP: int = 600  # judge-proposed edited-syndication band (NOT scored)
101	
102	DEFAULT_SEED: int = 20260623
103	
104	VALID_LABELS: Tuple[str, ...] = ("syndicated_copy", "distinct")
105	VALID_LABEL_SOURCES: Tuple[str, ...] = (
106	    "canonical_body",
107	    "canonical_url_refetch",
108	    "canonical_doi",
109	    "cross_topic",
110	    "curated_hard_negative",
111	)
112	
113	
114	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
115	# Data model.
116	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
117	
118	@dataclass
119	class Member:
120	    """One side of a candidate pair — carries its stable provenance IDs (KEEP-ALL)."""
121	
122	    member_id: str  # globally-unique stable id: f"{slug}::{run_tag}::{evidence_id}"
123	    slug: str
124	    run_tag: str
125	    evidence_id: str
126	    source_url: str
127	    doi: str
128	    title: str
129	    body: str  # normalized body text the dedup candidate actually compares
130	    body_sha: str
131	
132	    def to_provenance(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
133	        return {
134	            "member_id": self.member_id,
135	            "slug": self.slug,
136	            "run_tag": self.run_tag,
137	            "evidence_id": self.evidence_id,
138	            "source_url": self.source_url,
139	            "doi": self.doi,
140	            "title": self.title,
141	            "body_sha": self.body_sha,
142	        }
143	
144	
145	@dataclass
146	class LabeledPair:
147	    pair_id: str
148	    a: Dict[str, Any]  # member provenance (no body text — bodies live in the side index)
149	    b: Dict[str, Any]
150	    label: str  # "syndicated_copy" | "distinct"
151	    label_source: str
152	    rationale: str = ""
153	
154	
155	@dataclass
156	class FixtureManifest:
157	    seed: int
158	    snapshots_pooled: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
159	    snapshots_requested: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
160	    snapshots_missing: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
161	    n_members: int = 0
162	    n_exact_positive_pairs: int = 0
163	    n_cross_topic_negative_pairs: int = 0
164	    n_curated_hard_negative_pairs: int = 0
165	    n_scored_pairs: int = 0
166	    n_pending_adjudication: int = 0
167	    exhaustive: bool = False
168	    sampling_bound_note: str = ""
169	    flags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
170	
171	
172	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
173	# Normalization (shared, deterministic — NOT any candidate's similarity).
174	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
175	
176	def normalize_body(text: str) -> str:
177	    """Lowercase, strip punctuation, collapse whitespace. Identity normalization only.
178	
179	    This is the canonical-identity body key. It is intentionally simple and is NOT a
180	    similarity measure — two bodies share a key iff they are byte-identical after trivial
181	    normalization, which is a ground-truth identity signal, not a learned/threshold score.
182	    """
183	    if not text:
184	        return ""
185	    t = text.lower()
186	    t = re.sub(r"[^\w\s]", " ", t)
187	    return " ".join(t.split())
188	
189	
190	def sha256_text(text: str) -> str:
191	    return hashlib.sha256((text or "").encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
192	
193	
194	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
195	# Snapshot loading.
196	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
197	
198	def _run_tag_for_path(path: str) -> str:
199	    """Stable short tag for the run dir a snapshot came from (for provenance + dup collapse)."""
200	    norm = path.replace("\\", "/")
201	    parts = [p for p in norm.split("/") if p]
202	    try:
203	        i = parts.index("outputs")
204	        tag = parts[i + 1] if i + 1 < len(parts) else "outputs_root"
205	    except ValueError:
206	        tag = parts[-3] if len(parts) >= 3 else "unknown_run"
207	    return re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_]+", "_", tag)[:48] or "run"
208	
209	
210	def discover_snapshots(repo_root: str, slugs: Tuple[str, ...]) -> Dict[str, List[str]]:
211	    """Find every corpus_snapshot.json for each slug, anywhere under outputs/.
212	
213	    Returns slug -> sorted list of file paths.
214	    """
215	    out: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
216	    outputs = os.path.join(repo_root, "outputs")
217	    for slug in slugs:
218	        hits = sorted(
219	            set(
220	                glob.glob(
221	                    os.path.join(outputs, "**", slug, "corpus_snapshot.json"), recursive=True
222	                )
223	            )
224	        )
225	        out[slug] = hits
226	    return out
227	
228	
229	def load_members_from_snapshot(path: str, slug: str) -> List[Member]:
230	    """Load eligible members (rows with a real normalized body) from one snapshot."""
231	    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
232	        data = json.load(handle)
233	    return members_from_rows(data.get("evidence_for_gen", []) or [], slug, _run_tag_for_path(path))
234	
235	
236	def members_from_rows(rows: List[Dict[str, Any]], slug: str, run_tag: str) -> List[Member]:
237	    """Build Member objects from evidence rows (split out so the smoke test can call it)."""
238	    members: List[Member] = []
239	    for row in rows:
240	        raw_body = row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or ""
241	        body = normalize_body(raw_body)
242	        if len(body) < MIN_BODY_CHARS:
243	            continue
244	        evidence_id = str(row.get("evidence_id") or row.get("v30_entity_id") or "")
245	        if not evidence_id:
246	            continue
247	        source_url = str(row.get("source_url") or "")
248	        members.append(
249	            Member(
250	                member_id=f"{slug}::{run_tag}::{evidence_id}",
251	                slug=slug,
252	                run_tag=run_tag,
253	                evidence_id=evidence_id,
254	                source_url=source_url,
255	                doi=str(row.get("doi") or "").strip().lower(),
256	                title=str(row.get("title") or ""),
257	                body=body,
258	                body_sha=sha256_text(body),
259	            )
260	        )
261	    return members
262	
263	
264	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
265	# Pair construction by CANONICAL IDENTITY (anti-circularity core).
266	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
267	
268	def _pair_key(a: Member, b: Member) -> Tuple[str, str]:
269	    return tuple(sorted((a.member_id, b.member_id)))  # type: ignore[return-value]
270	
271	
272	def build_exact_positive_pairs(members: List[Member]) -> List[Tuple[Member, Member, str]]:
273	    """syndicated_copy positives from canonical identity ONLY.
274	
275	    Three independent identity channels, none of which is a dedup-candidate similarity:
276	      - canonical_body         : identical normalized-body sha, different source_url
277	      - canonical_url_refetch  : identical normalized-body sha, same source_url across runs
278	      - canonical_doi          : identical (non-empty) DOI, different source_url
279	    A pair qualifies if ANY channel fires; label_source records the channel.
280	    """
281	    by_body: Dict[str, List[Member]] = {}
282	    by_doi: Dict[str, List[Member]] = {}
283	    for m in members:
284	        by_body.setdefault(m.body_sha, []).append(m)
285	        if m.doi:
286	            by_doi.setdefault(m.doi, []).append(m)
287	
288	    seen: set = set()
289	    pairs: List[Tuple[Member, Member, str]] = []
290	
291	    for _sha, group in by_body.items():
292	        if len(group) < 2:
293	            continue
294	        for a, b in itertools.combinations(group, 2):
295	            if a.member_id == b.member_id:
296	                continue
297	            k = _pair_key(a, b)
298	            if k in seen:
299	                continue
300	            seen.add(k)
301	            same_url = bool(a.source_url) and a.source_url == b.source_url
302	            src = "canonical_url_refetch" if same_url else "canonical_body"
303	            pairs.append((a, b, src))
304	
305	    for _doi, group in by_doi.items():
306	        if len(group) < 2:
307	            continue
308	        for a, b in itertools.combinations(group, 2):
309	            if a.member_id == b.member_id or a.body_sha == b.body_sha:
310	                continue  # body channel already covered identical bodies
311	            k = _pair_key(a, b)
312	            if k in seen:
313	                continue
314	            seen.add(k)
315	            pairs.append((a, b, "canonical_doi"))
316	
317	    return pairs
318	
319	
320	def build_cross_topic_negatives(
321	    members_by_slug: Dict[str, List[Member]],
322	    rng: random.Random,
323	    target: int,
324	) -> List[Tuple[Member, Member, str]]:
325	    """distinct negatives from DISJOINT-topic pairs (different questions = distinct universes).
326	
327	    Sampled (cross-topic space is enormous), seeded.
328	    """
329	    slugs = [s for s, ms in members_by_slug.items() if ms]
330	    pairs: List[Tuple[Member, Member, str]] = []
331	    if len(slugs) < 2:
332	        return pairs
333	    seen: set = set()
334	    attempts = 0
335	    max_attempts = max(target * 50, 1000)
336	    while len(pairs) < target and attempts < max_attempts:
337	        attempts += 1
338	        s1, s2 = rng.sample(slugs, 2)
339	        a = rng.choice(members_by_slug[s1])
340	        b = rng.choice(members_by_slug[s2])
341	        if a.body_sha == b.body_sha:
342	            continue  # cross-topic but identical boilerplate -> not a clean negative
343	        k = _pair_key(a, b)
344	        if k in seen:
345	            continue
346	        seen.add(k)
347	        pairs.append((a, b, "cross_topic"))
348	    return pairs
349	
350	
351	def build_pending_adjudication(
352	    members: List[Member],
353	    rng: random.Random,
354	    target: int,
355	) -> List[Tuple[Member, Member]]:
356	    """Edited-syndication band: SAME source_url across runs, body NOT identical.
357	
358	    Judge-PROPOSED near-dups (same page, fetch jitter / truncation): very likely
359	    syndicated_copy but NOT auto-scored, because confirming requires reading the bodies
360	    (two-family adjudication). Emitted to pending_adjudication.jsonl; never a scored label.
361	    """
362	    by_url: Dict[str, List[Member]] = {}
363	    for m in members:
364	        if m.source_url:
365	            by_url.setdefault(m.source_url, []).append(m)
366	    candidates: List[Tuple[Member, Member]] = []
367	    seen: set = set()
368	    for _url, group in by_url.items():
369	        if len(group) < 2:
370	            continue
371	        for a, b in itertools.combinations(group, 2):
372	            if a.body_sha == b.body_sha:
373	                continue  # identical body already a scored EXACT positive
374	            k = _pair_key(a, b)
375	            if k in seen:
376	                continue
377	            seen.add(k)
378	            candidates.append((a, b))
379	    rng.shuffle(candidates)
380	    return candidates[:target]
381	
382	
383	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
384	# Curated hard-negative seeds (the authored, human/two-family part).
385	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
386	
387	def load_or_init_hard_negative_seeds(out_dir: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
388	    """Load curated distinct-but-similar seed pairs; author a starter set if absent.
389	
390	    Each seed is a REAL distinct pair that a naive similarity would over-merge, with a written
391	    "why distinct" rationale. This is the clinical-safety hard-negative band the precision
392	    floor is really measured on. These are AUTHORED labels (label_source =
393	    curated_hard_negative), never derived from any candidate score. The starter seeds are
394	    method-agnostic distinct pairs (two independent RCTs, review-vs-primary, two guideline
395	    editions, two agency labels differing in one word). The live build on the GPU box
396	    augments these with snapshot-resolved real pairs under two-family adjudication.
397	    """
398	    path = os.path.join(out_dir, "hard_negative_seeds.jsonl")
399	    if os.path.isfile(path):
400	        seeds: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
401	        with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
402	            for line in handle:
403	                line = line.strip()
404	                if line:
405	                    seeds.append(json.loads(line))
406	        if seeds:
407	            return seeds
408	    seeds = starter_hard_negative_seeds()
409	    with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
410	        for s in seeds:
411	            handle.write(json.dumps(s, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
412	    return seeds
413	
414	
415	def starter_hard_negative_seeds() -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
416	    """Authored distinct-but-similar pairs with written rationales (template seed set)."""
417	    return [
418	        {
419	            "seed_id": "hn_two_independent_rcts",
420	            "body_a": normalize_body(
421	                "In a randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial of 240 patients with "
422	                "stage II colorectal cancer, the synbiotic arm showed a statistically "
423	                "significant reduction in proliferation markers at 12 weeks (p=0.03)."
424	            ),
425	            "body_b": normalize_body(
426	                "In a randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial of 198 patients with "
427	                "stage III colorectal cancer, the probiotic arm showed a statistically "
428	                "significant reduction in inflammatory cytokines at 8 weeks (p=0.02)."
429	            ),
430	            "label": "distinct",
431	            "label_source": "curated_hard_negative",
432	            "rationale": "Two SEPARATE trials: different sample sizes (240 vs 198), disease "
433	            "stage (II vs III), intervention (synbiotic vs probiotic), endpoint (proliferation "
434	            "vs cytokines), timepoint and p-value. Boilerplate RCT phrasing overlaps but the "
435	            "studies are independent corroborators; merging them DELETES a real corroborating "
436	            "source (a §-1.3 violation).",
437	        },
438	        {
439	            "seed_id": "hn_review_vs_included_primary",
440	            "body_a": normalize_body(
441	                "This systematic review and meta-analysis pooled 14 cohort studies "
442	                "(n=52,300) and found a relative risk of 1.4 for the exposure."
443	            ),
444	            "body_b": normalize_body(
445	                "This prospective cohort study of 3,100 participants reported a relative risk "
446	                "of 1.4 for the exposure over a 9-year follow-up."
447	            ),
448	            "label": "distinct",
449	            "label_source": "curated_hard_negative",
450	            "rationale": "A meta-analysis and ONE of its included primary cohorts. Same RR (1.4) "
451	            "and shared vocabulary, but distinct evidence levels (pooled review vs single "
452	            "cohort). Collapsing them loses the review-vs-primary distinction the basket "
453	            "weighting depends on.",
454	        },
455	        {
456	            "seed_id": "hn_same_guideline_two_editions",
457	            "body_a": normalize_body(
458	                "The 2021 clinical practice guideline recommends first-line therapy X for "
459	                "newly diagnosed patients, with a strong recommendation and moderate-quality "
460	                "evidence."
461	            ),
462	            "body_b": normalize_body(
463	                "The 2024 clinical practice guideline updates the recommendation to first-line "
464	                "therapy X for newly diagnosed patients, now with a strong recommendation and "
465	                "high-quality evidence after new trial data."
466	            ),
467	            "label": "distinct",
468	            "label_source": "curated_hard_negative",
469	            "rationale": "Two EDITIONS of the same guideline (2021 vs 2024). Near-identical "
470	            "wording, but the evidence grade changed (moderate -> high) on new data; the "
471	            "editions are distinct authoritative sources. Merging hides the temporal update a "
472	            "clinician must see.",
473	        },
474	        {
475	            "seed_id": "hn_two_agency_labels_same_drug",
476	            "body_a": normalize_body(
477	                "The product label states the drug is indicated for adults with the condition "
478	                "and is contraindicated in patients with severe hepatic impairment."
479	            ),
480	            "body_b": normalize_body(
481	                "The product label states the drug is indicated for adults with the condition "
482	                "and is contraindicated in patients with severe renal impairment."
483	            ),
484	            "label": "distinct",
485	            "label_source": "curated_hard_negative",
486	            "rationale": "Two regulatory labels for the same drug differing in ONE word "
487	            "(hepatic vs renal contraindication). Extremely high lexical overlap, but the "
488	            "safety content differs — exactly the case where a false merge is clinically "
489	            "dangerous. Must stay distinct.",
490	        },
491	    ]

494	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
495	# Fixture assembly.
496	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
497	
498	def assemble_fixture(
499	    repo_root: str,
500	    out_dir: str,
501	    seed: int = DEFAULT_SEED,
502	    slugs: Tuple[str, ...] = CANONICAL_SLUGS,
503	) -> FixtureManifest:
504	    os.makedirs(out_dir, exist_ok=True)
505	    rng = random.Random(seed)
506	
507	    discovered = discover_snapshots(repo_root, slugs)
508	    members_by_slug: Dict[str, List[Member]] = {}
509	    pooled_snapshots: List[str] = []
510	    missing: List[str] = []
511	    seen_member_ids: set = set()
512	
513	    for slug, paths in discovered.items():
514	        if not paths:
515	            missing.append(slug)
516	            continue
517	        slug_members: List[Member] = []
518	        for path in paths:
519	            try:
520	                ms = load_members_from_snapshot(path, slug)
521	            except (OSError, ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError):
522	                continue
523	            pooled_snapshots.append(os.path.relpath(path, repo_root).replace("\\", "/"))
524	            for m in ms:
525	                if m.member_id in seen_member_ids:
526	                    continue  # same evidence_id in same run dir -> keep one stable member
527	                seen_member_ids.add(m.member_id)
528	                slug_members.append(m)
529	        if slug_members:
530	            members_by_slug[slug] = slug_members
531	
532	    all_members: List[Member] = [m for ms in members_by_slug.values() for m in ms]
533	    manifest = build_pairs_into_files(
534	        members_by_slug, all_members, out_dir, rng, seed, slugs, pooled_snapshots, missing
535	    )
536	    return manifest
537	
538	
539	def build_pairs_into_files(
540	    members_by_slug: Dict[str, List[Member]],
541	    all_members: List[Member],
542	    out_dir: str,
543	    rng: random.Random,
544	    seed: int,
545	    slugs: Tuple[str, ...],
546	    pooled_snapshots: List[str],
547	    missing: List[str],
548	) -> FixtureManifest:
549	    """Construct + write all fixture artifacts. Split out so the smoke test can drive it on
550	    synthetic members without touching the filesystem snapshots."""
551	    manifest = FixtureManifest(
552	        seed=seed,
553	        snapshots_pooled=pooled_snapshots,
554	        snapshots_requested=list(slugs),
555	        snapshots_missing=missing,
556	        n_members=len(all_members),
557	    )
558	
559	    if not all_members:
560	        manifest.flags.append(
561	            "FATAL: zero eligible members across all snapshots — cannot build a pair fixture. "
562	            "Verify outputs/corpus_backups/extracted/<slug>/corpus_snapshot.json exists."
563	        )
564	        _write_manifest(out_dir, manifest)
565	        raise RuntimeError(manifest.flags[-1])
566	
567	    if len(slugs) != 6:
568	        manifest.flags.append(
569	            f"NOTE: brief states '6 banked snapshots'; this build pooled "
570	            f"{len(members_by_slug)} slug(s) with data out of {len(slugs)} canonical slugs "
571	            f"requested. The drb_72 slug contributes many run-dir copies (the cross-run "
572	            f"re-fetch positive pool)."
573	        )
574	
575	    exact_pos = build_exact_positive_pairs(all_members)
576	    exhaustive = len(all_members) <= EXHAUSTIVE_MAX_BODIES
577	    if not exhaustive and len(exact_pos) > TARGET_EXACT_POSITIVES:
578	        rng.shuffle(exact_pos)
579	        exact_pos = exact_pos[:TARGET_EXACT_POSITIVES]
580	
581	    cross_neg = build_cross_topic_negatives(members_by_slug, rng, TARGET_CROSS_TOPIC_NEGATIVES)
582	    hard_neg_seeds = load_or_init_hard_negative_seeds(out_dir)
583	    pending = build_pending_adjudication(all_members, rng, TARGET_PENDING_NEAR_DUP)
584	
585	    scored: List[LabeledPair] = []
586	    body_index: Dict[str, str] = {}
587	
588	    def _emit(a: Member, b: Member, label: str, label_source: str, rationale: str = "") -> None:
589	        body_index[a.member_id] = a.body
590	        body_index[b.member_id] = b.body
591	        pid = "pair_" + hashlib.sha1(
592	            (a.member_id + "||" + b.member_id + "||" + label_source).encode("utf-8")
593	        ).hexdigest()[:16]
594	        scored.append(
595	            LabeledPair(
596	                pair_id=pid,
597	                a=a.to_provenance(),
598	                b=b.to_provenance(),
599	                label=label,
600	                label_source=label_source,
601	                rationale=rationale,
602	            )
603	        )
604	
605	    for a, b, src in exact_pos:
606	        _emit(a, b, "syndicated_copy", src)
607	    for a, b, src in cross_neg:
608	        _emit(a, b, "distinct", src)
609	
610	    for seed_row in hard_neg_seeds:
611	        ma = Member(
612	            member_id="seed::" + seed_row["seed_id"] + "::a",
613	            slug="curated",
614	            run_tag="seed",
615	            evidence_id=seed_row["seed_id"] + "_a",
616	            source_url="",
617	            doi="",
618	            title="",
619	            body=seed_row["body_a"],
620	            body_sha=sha256_text(seed_row["body_a"]),
621	        )
622	        mb = Member(
623	            member_id="seed::" + seed_row["seed_id"] + "::b",
624	            slug="curated",
625	            run_tag="seed",
626	            evidence_id=seed_row["seed_id"] + "_b",
627	            source_url="",
628	            doi="",
629	            title="",
630	            body=seed_row["body_b"],
631	            body_sha=sha256_text(seed_row["body_b"]),
632	        )
633	        _emit(ma, mb, "distinct", "curated_hard_negative", seed_row.get("rationale", ""))
634	
635	    manifest.n_exact_positive_pairs = sum(1 for p in scored if p.label == "syndicated_copy")
636	    manifest.n_cross_topic_negative_pairs = sum(
637	        1 for p in scored if p.label_source == "cross_topic"
638	    )
639	    manifest.n_curated_hard_negative_pairs = sum(
640	        1 for p in scored if p.label_source == "curated_hard_negative"
641	    )
642	    manifest.n_scored_pairs = len(scored)
643	    manifest.n_pending_adjudication = len(pending)
644	    manifest.exhaustive = exhaustive
645	    manifest.sampling_bound_note = (
646	        "exhaustive C(N,2) over identity-grouped members"
647	        if exhaustive
648	        else (
649	            f"stratified seeded sampling: exact_pos capped {TARGET_EXACT_POSITIVES}, "
650	            f"cross_topic_neg target {TARGET_CROSS_TOPIC_NEGATIVES}, pending near-dup target "
651	            f"{TARGET_PENDING_NEAR_DUP}; seed={seed}. The precision floor is estimated on the "
652	            f"hard-negative + cross-topic bands; run_bakeoff reports a Wilson CI so a thin band "
653	            f"does not silently crown a winner."
654	        )
655	    )
656	    if manifest.n_exact_positive_pairs == 0:
657	        manifest.flags.append(
658	            "WARNING: zero canonical-identity positives — recall is unmeasurable. Check that "
659	            "cross-run re-fetch snapshots are present (the drb_72 run dirs)."
660	        )
661	    if manifest.n_curated_hard_negative_pairs < 4:
662	        manifest.flags.append(
663	            "WARNING: hard-negative band is thin; the 0.97 precision floor decision will have "
664	            "a wide CI. Author more curated_hard_negative seeds before crowning a winner."
665	        )
666	
667	    _write_jsonl(os.path.join(out_dir, "dedup_pairs_fixture.jsonl"), [asdict(p) for p in scored])
668	    _write_jsonl(
669	        os.path.join(out_dir, "pending_adjudication.jsonl"),
670	        [
671	            {
672	                "pair_id": "pending_"
673	                + hashlib.sha1((a.member_id + "||" + b.member_id).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[
674	                    :16
675	                ],
676	                "a": a.to_provenance(),
677	                "b": b.to_provenance(),
678	                "proposed_label": "syndicated_copy",
679	                "label_status": "PENDING_TWO_FAMILY_ADJUDICATION",
680	                "note": "same source_url re-fetched across runs, body differs (fetch jitter / "
681	                "truncation). Judge-PROPOSED only; NOT a scored label. run_bakeoff.py refuses "
682	                "to score these until a two-family-adjudicated artifact resolves them.",
683	            }
684	            for a, b in pending
685	        ],
686	    )
687	    _write_json(os.path.join(out_dir, "dedup_member_bodies.json"), body_index)
688	    _write_manifest(out_dir, manifest)
689	    return manifest
690	
691	
692	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
693	# IO helpers.
694	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
695	
696	def _write_jsonl(path: str, rows: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
697	    with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
698	        for row in rows:
699	            handle.write(json.dumps(row, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
700	
701	
702	def _write_json(path: str, obj: Any) -> None:
703	    with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
704	        json.dump(obj, handle, ensure_ascii=False, indent=1)
705	
706	
707	def _write_manifest(out_dir: str, manifest: FixtureManifest) -> None:
708	    _write_json(os.path.join(out_dir, "dedup_fixture_manifest.json"), asdict(manifest))
709	
710	
711	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
712	# Loader used by run_bakeoff / gate0 (single source of truth for the fixture shape).
713	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
714	
715	def load_fixture(out_dir: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
716	    """Load the built fixture. Fails loud if any required artifact is missing or a scored
717	    label is not from a canonical-identity / authored source (anti-circularity guard)."""
718	    pairs_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "dedup_pairs_fixture.jsonl")
719	    bodies_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "dedup_member_bodies.json")
720	    manifest_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "dedup_fixture_manifest.json")
721	    for p in (pairs_path, bodies_path, manifest_path):
722	        if not os.path.isfile(p):
723	            raise FileNotFoundError(
724	                f"dedup fixture artifact missing: {p}. Run build_fixture.py first."
725	            )
726	    pairs: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
727	    with open(pairs_path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
728	        for line in handle:
729	            line = line.strip()
730	            if line:
731	                pairs.append(json.loads(line))
732	    with open(bodies_path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
733	        bodies = json.load(handle)
734	    with open(manifest_path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
735	        manifest = json.load(handle)
736	    assert_no_circular_labels(pairs)
737	    return {"pairs": pairs, "bodies": bodies, "manifest": manifest}
738	
739	
740	def assert_no_circular_labels(pairs: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
741	    """Fail loud if any scored pair has a non-binary label or a similarity-derived source."""
742	    for p in pairs:
743	        if p.get("label") not in VALID_LABELS:
744	            raise ValueError(f"fixture pair {p.get('pair_id')} has a non-binary scored label")
745	        if p.get("label_source") not in VALID_LABEL_SOURCES:
746	            raise ValueError(
747	                f"fixture pair {p.get('pair_id')} has a non-canonical label_source "
748	                f"{p.get('label_source')!r} — scored labels must come from canonical identity "
749	                f"or authored curation, never a candidate similarity (anti-circularity)."
750	            )
751	
752	
753	def main(argv: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> int:
754	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Build the dedup near-dup PAIR fixture.")
755	    parser.add_argument("--repo-root", default=os.environ.get("POLARIS_REPO_ROOT", "C:/POLARIS"))
756	    parser.add_argument(
757	        "--out-dir",
758	        default=os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)), "fixtures"),
759	    )
760	    parser.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=DEFAULT_SEED)
761	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
762	    manifest = assemble_fixture(args.repo_root, args.out_dir, seed=args.seed)
763	    print(json.dumps(asdict(manifest), indent=1))
764	    return 0
765	
766	
767	if __name__ == "__main__":
768	    raise SystemExit(main())
769

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-ret-002 (#1294) dedup layer — GATE-0 validity harness (the anti-drb_72 gate).
3	
4	No candidate score is trusted until BOTH canaries below are green. GATE-0 here has three teeth:
5	
6	  1. SCORER-MATH CANARY (known input -> known score). A hand-built tiny pair set with known
7	     gold and a FAKE candidate of known behaviour must produce the precomputed precision/recall.
8	     Catches a broken metric (e.g. precision/recall swapped, FP miscounted).
9	
10	  2. PER-CANDIDATE LIVENESS CANARY — BIDIRECTIONAL (the dedup-specific drb_72 trap). For each
11	     real candidate the harness asserts BOTH directions on the byte-identical controls and a
12	     clearly-distinct control:
13	       - byte-identical bodies  -> MUST merge          (else it is dead / no-op / load-failed)
14	       - clearly-distinct bodies-> MUST NOT merge       (else it over-merges everything)
15	     A stub / empty / load-fail / no-op candidate FAILS LOUD with a non-zero exit — it can
16	     NEVER score a believable-low number and pass. This is the most important test in the layer:
17	     a no-op merger scores PERFECT precision (TP/(TP+FP) with FP=0), so precision alone would
18	     pass it; the no-op RECALL floor + the must-merge direction kill it.
19	
20	  3. KEEP-ALL PROVENANCE CANARY. Clustering a small member set must conserve every member id
