1	"""I-ret-002 (#1294) layer 2 (fetch_crawl) — POLARIS repo-root resolver.
2	
3	WHY THIS EXISTS
4	---------------
5	These four bake-off files are authored in an isolated git worktree whose checkout does NOT
6	contain the production seams (``src/polaris_graph/retrieval/shell_detector.py``,
7	``scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py``) nor the banked corpus snapshots. They live only in
8	the shared POLARIS checkout. This helper resolves the real POLARIS repo root at runtime and
9	inserts it on ``sys.path`` so the authored files import the SAME seams the pipeline uses (no
10	fork, no copy) and read the SAME banked corpora — whether run from the worktree or, later,
11	from the main tree (where the root resolves to the local repo and nothing changes).
12	
13	RESOLUTION ORDER (first hit wins; FAILS LOUD if none found — never a silent wrong root):
14	  1. ``$POLARIS_ROOT`` env var, if it points at a dir containing the seams.
15	  2. The conventional absolute install path ``C:/POLARIS`` (and ``/POLARIS`` on POSIX).
16	  3. Walk up from this file's directory until a dir containing the seams is found.
17	
18	No magic numbers; no silent fallback — a missing root raises so the harness fails loud (LAW II).
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import os
24	import sys
25	
26	# Marker files that uniquely identify a real POLARIS checkout (the seams this layer reuses).
27	_ROOT_MARKERS = (
28	    os.path.join("src", "polaris_graph", "retrieval", "shell_detector.py"),
29	    os.path.join("scripts", "dr_benchmark", "gate0_lineage.py"),
30	)
31	
32	
33	def _has_markers(candidate: str) -> bool:
34	    return bool(candidate) and all(
35	        os.path.isfile(os.path.join(candidate, m)) for m in _ROOT_MARKERS
36	    )
37	
38	
39	def _candidate_paths() -> list[str]:
40	    candidates: list[str] = []
41	    env = os.environ.get("POLARIS_ROOT", "").strip()
42	    if env:
43	        candidates.append(os.path.abspath(env))
44	    # Conventional install paths.
45	    candidates.append(os.path.abspath("C:/POLARIS"))
46	    candidates.append(os.path.abspath("/POLARIS"))
47	    # Walk up from this file (covers the main-tree case: the seams are local).
48	    here = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
49	    cur = here
50	    for _ in range(12):
51	        candidates.append(cur)
52	        parent = os.path.dirname(cur)
53	        if parent == cur:
54	            break
55	        cur = parent
56	    # De-dup, preserve order.
57	    seen: set[str] = set()
58	    ordered: list[str] = []
59	    for c in candidates:
60	        if c and c not in seen:
61	            seen.add(c)
62	            ordered.append(c)
63	    return ordered
64	
65	
66	def resolve_polaris_root() -> str:
67	    """Return the absolute POLARIS repo root, or raise RuntimeError (FAIL LOUD)."""
68	    for candidate in _candidate_paths():
69	        if _has_markers(candidate):
70	            return candidate
71	    raise RuntimeError(
72	        "fetch_crawl bake-off: could not resolve the POLARIS repo root (no candidate dir "
73	        f"contained the seams {_ROOT_MARKERS!r}). Set POLARIS_ROOT to the real checkout, or run "
74	        "from inside the POLARIS tree. FAIL LOUD — never import a forked/missing seam silently."
75	    )
76	
77	
78	def ensure_on_syspath() -> str:
79	    """Resolve the POLARIS root and ensure it is importable; return the root. Idempotent."""
80	    root = resolve_polaris_root()
81	    if root not in sys.path:
82	        sys.path.insert(0, root)
83	    return root
84

1	"""I-ret-002 (#1294) layer 2 (fetch_crawl) — labeled ground-truth fixture builder.
2	
3	WHAT THIS BUILDS
4	----------------
5	``fetch_crawl_refbody_fixture.jsonl`` — ~60-100 URLs stratified across source-types
6	(OA / paywalled / gov / news / social) drawn from the 6 banked corpus snapshots. Each
7	fixture row carries:
8	
9	  * ``url``                 — the source URL that was fetched on the real run.
10	  * ``source_type``         — stratification bucket (oa / paywalled / gov / news / social).
11	  * ``tier``                — the recorded credibility tier (T1..T7 / UNKNOWN) — a WEIGHT
12	                              surfaced for analysis, NEVER a drop filter (§-1.3).
13	  * ``reference_body``      — the labeled main-content reference body (the real fetched
14	                              ``direct_quote`` that the run actually grounded against).
15	  * ``reference_tokens``    — content-word token SET of ``reference_body`` (the recall target;
16	                              we score reference-recall against this SET, never a length floor).
17	  * ``recovery_class``      — the per-URL recovery verdict the engine OUGHT to produce on a
18	                              healthy fetch: RECOVERED / WALLED / SOFT_STUB / FETCH_FAIL.
19	  * ``recovery_class_proposed_by`` — provenance of the recovery_class label (the deterministic
20	                              shell-vocabulary rubric; a judge may PROPOSE only, never set it).
21	
22	WHY THE SCORED LABEL IS NOT JUDGE-TASTE (Codex anti-pattern guard)
23	-----------------------------------------------------------------
24	The SCORED ``recovery_class`` is set by a PRE-REGISTERED OBJECTIVE rubric (the shared
25	``shell_detector`` vocabulary + an empty-body test), NOT by "reads like a stub". The
26	``shell_detector`` is the production fetch-shell engine (I-beatboth-001 #1276) — reusing it
27	here means the fixture label matches the exact deterministic signal the pipeline uses, so a
28	RECOVERED row is one whose body is NOT a known shell vocabulary AND is non-empty. A two-family
29	adjudication HOOK (``--emit-adjudication``) writes a side file the operator / Codex can
30	spot-check; the judge / LLM never sets the scored label (faithfulness invariant: PROPOSE only).
31	
32	REUSE (per brief): ``src.polaris_graph.retrieval.shell_detector`` for the recovery vocabulary,
33	banked ``state/reserved_corpus_snapshots`` + ``outputs/corpus_backups/extracted`` snapshots for
34	the labeled reference bodies. The POLARIS repo root is resolved via ``_polaris_root`` so the
35	worktree-authored file imports the SAME production seam (no fork).
36	
37	NO §-1.1 BANNED PROXY: the fixture stores the reference-token SET as the recall target, not a
38	word/char count. ``recovery_class`` is a verdict, not a count. ``tier`` is a surfaced weight.
39	"""
40	
41	from __future__ import annotations
42	
43	import argparse
44	import json
45	import os
46	import re
47	from dataclasses import dataclass
48	from typing import Any, Iterable
49	
50	# Resolve the real POLARIS root and put it on sys.path BEFORE importing the production seams.
51	from _polaris_root import ensure_on_syspath
52	
53	_POLARIS_ROOT = ensure_on_syspath()
54	
55	# shell_detector is the single-source-of-truth fetch-shell vocabulary (LAW V). We import the
56	# RECOVERY-CLASS rubric from it so the fixture label is the exact deterministic signal prod uses.
57	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.shell_detector import (  # noqa: E402  (after sys.path setup)
58	    is_access_denial_stub,
59	    is_cited_span_shell,
60	)
61	
62	# ── Constants (LAW VI: no magic numbers buried in logic — all named / env-overridable) ──
63	
64	# Recovery-class verdict vocabulary (the metric's label space — a verdict, not a count).
65	RECOVERED = "RECOVERED"
66	WALLED = "WALLED"
67	SOFT_STUB = "SOFT_STUB"
68	FETCH_FAIL = "FETCH_FAIL"
69	RECOVERY_CLASSES = (RECOVERED, WALLED, SOFT_STUB, FETCH_FAIL)
70	
71	# Source-type stratification buckets.
72	SOURCE_TYPES = ("oa", "paywalled", "gov", "news", "social")
73	
74	# Banked corpus-snapshot roots (read-only inputs), relative to the resolved POLARIS root.
75	_DEFAULT_SNAPSHOT_SUBDIRS = (
76	    os.path.join("state", "reserved_corpus_snapshots"),
77	    os.path.join("outputs", "corpus_backups", "extracted"),
78	)
79	
80	# Default fixture output path (this layer's directory only — no cross-layer writes).
81	_DEFAULT_OUT = os.path.join(
82	    os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)),
83	    "fetch_crawl_refbody_fixture.jsonl",
84	)
85	
86	# Stratification target band (brief: ~60-100 URLs). Env-overridable.
87	_ENV_TARGET_MIN = "PG_FETCH_FIXTURE_MIN_URLS"
88	_ENV_TARGET_MAX = "PG_FETCH_FIXTURE_MAX_URLS"
89	_DEFAULT_TARGET_MIN = 60
90	_DEFAULT_TARGET_MAX = 100
91	
92	# A body shorter than this (after strip) and not a shell is a SOFT_STUB (truncated / abstract-only
93	# recovery), per the WebMainBench labelling protocol — a *recovery class*, NOT a quality length
94	# floor: a healthy long article is RECOVERED, a near-empty non-shell stub is SOFT_STUB.
95	_ENV_SOFT_STUB_MAX = "PG_FETCH_FIXTURE_SOFT_STUB_MAX_CHARS"
96	_DEFAULT_SOFT_STUB_MAX = 400
97	
98	# Content-word token regex (drop punctuation; keep alphanumerics incl. unicode).
99	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[^\W\d_]+|\d+", re.UNICODE)
100	
101	# A tiny stop-set so reference-recall measures CONTENT overlap, not function-word overlap.
102	_STOPWORDS = frozenset(
103	    """a an and are as at be by for from has have in is it its of on or that the to was were
104	    will with this these those we they their there here""".split()
105	)
106	
107	
108	@dataclass
109	class FixtureRow:
110	    """One labeled fetch_crawl ground-truth row."""
111	
112	    url: str
113	    source_type: str
114	    tier: str
115	    reference_body: str
116	    recovery_class: str
117	    recovery_class_proposed_by: str = "shell_detector_rubric_v1"
118	    slug: str = ""
119	    evidence_id: str = ""
120	    doi: str = ""
121	    provenance_class: str = ""
122	
123	    def reference_tokens(self) -> list[str]:
124	        """Content-word token list of the reference body (the recall target SET source)."""
125	        return content_tokens(self.reference_body)
126	
127	    def reference_trustworthy(self) -> bool:
128	        """True iff the reference body is trustworthy main content (gold == RECOVERED).
129	
130	        Only on a RECOVERED row did the INCUMBENT get full text — so only there is the reference
131	        body a valid recall target. A WALLED / SOFT_STUB / FETCH_FAIL gold row is one the incumbent
132	        FAILED on; its body is a shell, so reference-recall vs it is NOT used to judge an engine
133	        (the scorer carries this flag to avoid the §-1.3 inversion of punishing a wall-breaker).
134	        """
135	        return self.recovery_class == RECOVERED
136	
137	    def boundary_for_adjudication(self) -> bool:
138	        """True iff this SOFT_STUB row sits NEAR the length ceiling — a judge should confirm it.
139	
140	        The SOFT_STUB-vs-RECOVERED split is decided by a length ceiling at the LABELING layer (not
141	        the metric). Rows whose body length is within a margin of the ceiling are the ambiguous ones
142	        (a borderline-short real article vs a truncated stub); they are flagged so the two-family
143	        adjudication hook surfaces them for judge confirmation rather than trusting a bare length cut.
144	        """
145	        if self.recovery_class != SOFT_STUB:
146	            return False
147	        ceiling = _soft_stub_max_chars()
148	        body_len = len((self.reference_body or "").strip())
149	        margin = max(50, ceiling // 4)
150	        return abs(body_len - ceiling) <= margin
151	
152	    def to_record(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
153	        toks = sorted(set(self.reference_tokens()))
154	        return {
155	            "url": self.url,
156	            "source_type": self.source_type,
157	            "tier": self.tier,
158	            "reference_body": self.reference_body,
159	            "reference_tokens": toks,
160	            "recovery_class": self.recovery_class,
161	            "reference_trustworthy": self.reference_trustworthy(),
162	            "boundary_for_adjudication": self.boundary_for_adjudication(),
163	            "recovery_class_proposed_by": self.recovery_class_proposed_by,
164	            "slug": self.slug,
165	            "evidence_id": self.evidence_id,
166	            "doi": self.doi,
167	            "provenance_class": self.provenance_class,
168	        }
169	
170	
171	def content_tokens(text: str) -> list[str]:
172	    """Lowercase content-word tokens (stopwords removed). The recall target is the SET of these.
173	
174	    NOT a length proxy: this is the gold reference-token SET that engine output is recalled
175	    against (Jaccard / recall), exactly the §-1.1-compliant "real output vs labeled ground truth".
176	    """
177	    if not text:
178	        return []
179	    out: list[str] = []
180	    for m in _WORD_RE.findall(text.lower()):
181	        if len(m) <= 1:
182	            continue
183	        if m in _STOPWORDS:
184	            continue
185	        out.append(m)
186	    return out
187	
188	
189	def _soft_stub_max_chars() -> int:
190	    try:
191	        v = int(os.environ.get(_ENV_SOFT_STUB_MAX, _DEFAULT_SOFT_STUB_MAX) or _DEFAULT_SOFT_STUB_MAX)
192	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
193	        return _DEFAULT_SOFT_STUB_MAX
194	    return v if v > 0 else _DEFAULT_SOFT_STUB_MAX
195	
196	
197	def _target_band() -> tuple[int, int]:
198	    def _read(name: str, default: int) -> int:
199	        try:
200	            v = int(os.environ.get(name, default) or default)
201	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
202	            return default
203	        return v if v > 0 else default
204	
205	    lo = _read(_ENV_TARGET_MIN, _DEFAULT_TARGET_MIN)
206	    hi = _read(_ENV_TARGET_MAX, _DEFAULT_TARGET_MAX)
207	    return (lo, hi) if lo <= hi else (hi, lo)
208	
209	
210	# ── Recovery-class rubric (the PRE-REGISTERED OBJECTIVE label) ──────────────────────────
211	
212	def classify_recovery(body: str) -> str:
213	    """Deterministic recovery-class verdict for a fetched body (the SCORED label rubric).
214	
215	    PRE-REGISTERED OBJECTIVE rubric — judge / LLM never sets this (PROPOSE-only invariant):
216	      * empty / whitespace-only      -> FETCH_FAIL  (the fetch returned nothing).
217	      * a bot-wall / access-denial   -> WALLED      (CAPTCHA / Cloudflare / paywall interstitial).
218	      * any other known fetch-shell  -> SOFT_STUB   (cookie / 404 / language-nav / citation-UI chrome).
219	      * a non-shell but tiny body    -> SOFT_STUB   (truncated / abstract-only recovery).
220	      * a non-shell substantive body -> RECOVERED   (real main content).
221	
222	    This is the SAME shell vocabulary the production faithfulness gate uses (``shell_detector``),
223	    so a fixture label is never an opinion — it is the deterministic signal the pipeline trusts.
224	    """
225	    if body is None:
226	        return FETCH_FAIL
227	    stripped = body.strip()
228	    if not stripped:
229	        return FETCH_FAIL
230	    # Bot-wall / access-denial first (the WALLED class). is_access_denial_stub fires on the
231	    # unambiguous Cloudflare/CAPTCHA co-occurrence at any length and on short-body denial markers.
232	    if is_access_denial_stub(stripped):
233	        return WALLED
234	    # Any other fetch-shell (cookie banner, 404, language-nav, citation-UI/social chrome) -> SOFT_STUB.
235	    if is_cited_span_shell(stripped):
236	        return SOFT_STUB
237	    # A non-shell but near-empty body is a truncated / abstract-only recovery -> SOFT_STUB.
238	    if len(stripped) <= _soft_stub_max_chars():
239	        return SOFT_STUB
240	    return RECOVERED
241	
242	
243	# ── Source-type stratification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
244	
245	# Domain substrings -> source_type. Tier is the credibility WEIGHT; source_type is the FETCH
246	# difficulty stratum (a different axis). Necessary-but-not-sufficient: registered-domain alone.
247	_GOV_DOMAINS = (
248	    ".gov", ".gc.ca", "europa.eu", "ema.europa", "fda.gov", "nih.gov", "ncbi.nlm.nih.gov",
249	    "who.int", "nice.org.uk", "clinicaltrials.gov", "cdc.gov", "canada.ca", "tga.gov.au",
250	    "pmda.go.jp", "mhra", "health.gov",
251	)
252	_NEWS_DOMAINS = (
253	    "reuters.com", "bloomberg.com", "nytimes.com", "theguardian.com", "bbc.co", "wsj.com",
254	    "ft.com", "cnbc.com", "forbes.com", "apnews.com", "economist.com", "washingtonpost.com",
255	    "statnews.com", "medscape.com",
256	)
257	_SOCIAL_DOMAINS = (
258	    "twitter.com", "x.com", "reddit.com", "youtube.com", "facebook.com", "linkedin.com",
259	    "medium.com", "substack.com", "researchgate.net", "quora.com", "t.me",
260	)
261	# Paywalled-publisher domains (clinical journals that 403 the free cascade — Zyte's home turf).
262	_PAYWALL_DOMAINS = (
263	    "nejm.org", "thelancet.com", "jamanetwork.com", "sciencedirect.com", "springer.com",
264	    "wiley.com", "nature.com", "tandfonline.com", "sagepub.com", "oup.com", "bmj.com",
265	    "cell.com", "ahajournals.org", "academic.oup.com", "onlinelibrary.wiley.com",
266	)
267	
268	
269	def classify_source_type(url: str, provenance_class: str) -> str:
270	    """Map a URL to a fetch-difficulty stratum (oa / paywalled / gov / news / social).
271	
272	    Tier is NOT used here (tier is credibility weight; this is fetch-difficulty). Order matters:
273	    gov / social / news registered-domain checks first; paywalled-publisher domain next;
274	    open_access provenance or otherwise -> oa (the default easy fetch).
275	    """
276	    low = (url or "").lower()
277	    if any(d in low for d in _GOV_DOMAINS):
278	        return "gov"
279	    if any(d in low for d in _SOCIAL_DOMAINS):
280	        return "social"
281	    if any(d in low for d in _NEWS_DOMAINS):
282	        return "news"
283	    if any(d in low for d in _PAYWALL_DOMAINS):
284	        return "paywalled"
285	    if (provenance_class or "").strip().lower() == "open_access":
286	        return "oa"
287	    return "oa"
288	
289	
290	# ── Snapshot loading ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
291	
292	def _default_snapshot_roots() -> list[str]:
293	    return [os.path.join(_POLARIS_ROOT, sub) for sub in _DEFAULT_SNAPSHOT_SUBDIRS]
294	
295	
296	def _iter_snapshot_files(roots: Iterable[str]) -> list[str]:
297	    """Enumerate every *corpus_snapshot.json under the given roots."""
298	    files: list[str] = []
299	    for root in roots:
300	        if not os.path.isdir(root):
301	            continue
302	        for dirpath, _dirnames, filenames in os.walk(root):
303	            for name in filenames:
304	                if name.endswith("corpus_snapshot.json"):
305	                    files.append(os.path.join(dirpath, name))
306	    return sorted(set(files))
307	
308	
309	def _evidence_rows(snapshot_path: str) -> tuple[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]:
310	    """Return (slug, evidence_for_gen rows) for a snapshot; ('', []) on any read error."""
311	    try:
312	        with open(snapshot_path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
313	            data = json.load(handle)
314	    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
315	        return "", []
316	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
317	        return "", []
318	    slug = str(data.get("slug") or "")
319	    rows = data.get("evidence_for_gen")
320	    if not isinstance(rows, list):
321	        return slug, []
322	    return slug, [r for r in rows if isinstance(r, dict)]
323	
324	
325	# ── Fixture build ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
326	
327	def build_rows(roots: Iterable[str] | None = None) -> list[FixtureRow]:
328	    """Build the stratified labeled fixture rows from banked snapshots.
329	
330	    Dedups by URL (the first occurrence of a URL wins). Caps per (source_type) bucket so the
331	    fixture is stratified, not OA-dominated. FAILS LOUD (raises) if no snapshot rows are found,
332	    so a silent-empty fixture can never slip through (§-1.1 / LAW II).
333	    """
334	    roots = list(roots) if roots is not None else _default_snapshot_roots()
335	    files = _iter_snapshot_files(roots)
336	    if not files:
337	        raise RuntimeError(
338	            f"build_fixture: no corpus_snapshot.json found under {roots!r} — cannot build a "
339	            f"labeled fetch_crawl fixture from banked corpora (FAIL LOUD, never an empty fixture)."
340	        )
341	
342	    target_min, target_max = _target_band()
343	    # Per-bucket cap so 5 buckets * cap ~= target_max, with a floor so rarer buckets are not starved.
344	    per_bucket_cap = max(6, (target_max // max(1, len(SOURCE_TYPES))) + 4)
345	
346	    seen_urls: set[str] = set()
347	    by_bucket: dict[str, list[FixtureRow]] = {st: [] for st in SOURCE_TYPES}
348	
349	    for path in files:
350	        slug, rows = _evidence_rows(path)
351	        for r in rows:
352	            url = str(r.get("source_url") or "").strip()
353	            if not url.lower().startswith("http"):
354	                continue
355	            if url in seen_urls:
356	                continue
357	            body = str(r.get("direct_quote") or "")
358	            # Only label rows that carry a real fetched body (the reference body must exist to
359	            # be a recovery target). A row with no direct_quote cannot anchor reference-recall.
360	            if not body.strip():
361	                continue
362	            source_type = classify_source_type(url, str(r.get("provenance_class") or ""))
363	            bucket = by_bucket[source_type]
364	            if len(bucket) >= per_bucket_cap:
365	                continue
366	            seen_urls.add(url)
367	            bucket.append(
368	                FixtureRow(
369	                    url=url,
370	                    source_type=source_type,
371	                    tier=str(r.get("tier") or "UNKNOWN"),
372	                    reference_body=body,
373	                    recovery_class=classify_recovery(body),
374	                    slug=slug,
375	                    evidence_id=str(r.get("evidence_id") or ""),
376	                    doi=str(r.get("doi") or ""),
377	                    provenance_class=str(r.get("provenance_class") or ""),
378	                )
379	            )
380	
381	    rows_out: list[FixtureRow] = []
382	    for st in SOURCE_TYPES:
383	        rows_out.extend(by_bucket[st])
384	
385	    if not rows_out:
386	        raise RuntimeError(
387	            "build_fixture: every snapshot row was skipped (no http URL with a fetched body) — "
388	            "FAIL LOUD rather than emit an empty fetch_crawl fixture."
389	        )
390	    if len(rows_out) < target_min:
391	        # Honest under-build is allowed (banked data is finite) but is flagged, never hidden.
392	        print(
393	            f"[build_fixture] WARNING: built {len(rows_out)} rows < target_min {target_min}; "
394	            f"banked corpora yielded fewer unique fetched URLs than the target band "
395	            f"(stratification caps + dedup). Proceeding with the real available set."
396	        )
397	    return rows_out
398	
399	
400	def write_fixture(rows: list[FixtureRow], out_path: str) -> None:
401	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path), exist_ok=True)
402	    with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
403	        for row in rows:
404	            handle.write(json.dumps(row.to_record(), ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
405	
406	
407	def load_fixture(path: str | None = None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
408	    """Load the fixture JSONL into a list of record dicts. FAILS LOUD if the file is missing."""
409	    path = path or _DEFAULT_OUT
410	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
411	        raise FileNotFoundError(
412	            f"load_fixture: fetch_crawl fixture not found at {path} — run build_fixture.py first."
413	        )
414	    records: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
415	    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
416	        for line in handle:
417	            line = line.strip()
418	            if not line:
419	                continue
420	            records.append(json.loads(line))
421	    if not records:
422	        raise RuntimeError(f"load_fixture: fixture {path} is EMPTY — FAIL LOUD (never score on empty).")
423	    return records
424	
425	
426	def _stratification_summary(rows: list[FixtureRow]) -> dict[str, Any]:
427	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {st: 0 for st in SOURCE_TYPES}
428	    by_class: dict[str, int] = {c: 0 for c in RECOVERY_CLASSES}
429	    boundary = 0
430	    trustworthy = 0
431	    for r in rows:
432	        by_type[r.source_type] = by_type.get(r.source_type, 0) + 1
433	        by_class[r.recovery_class] = by_class.get(r.recovery_class, 0) + 1
434	        if r.boundary_for_adjudication():
435	            boundary += 1
436	        if r.reference_trustworthy():
437	            trustworthy += 1
438	    # Surface skew honestly (the advisor's note): the min/max source-type bucket sizes + the over/
439	    # under-target flag. Never hide an uneven build.
440	    counts = [v for v in by_type.values()]
441	    target_min, target_max = _target_band()
442	    return {
443	        "total": len(rows),
444	        "by_source_type": by_type,
445	        "by_recovery_class": by_class,
446	        "trustworthy_reference_rows": trustworthy,
447	        "boundary_rows_needing_adjudication": boundary,
448	        "source_type_min_bucket": min(counts) if counts else 0,
449	        "source_type_max_bucket": max(counts) if counts else 0,
450	        "over_target_band": len(rows) > target_max,
451	        "under_target_band": len(rows) < target_min,
452	        "target_band": [target_min, target_max],
453	    }
454	
455	
456	def _emit_adjudication(rows: list[FixtureRow], path: str) -> None:
457	    """Write the two-family adjudication side file (operator / Codex spot-check surface).
458	
459	    The judge / second family reviews the PROPOSED recovery_class here; the scored label in the
460	    fixture stays the deterministic rubric's. This is the PROPOSE-only hook (faithfulness invariant).
461	    """
462	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True)
463	    with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
464	        for r in rows:
465	            handle.write(
466	                json.dumps(
467	                    {
468	                        "url": r.url,
469	                        "source_type": r.source_type,
470	                        "tier": r.tier,
471	                        "proposed_recovery_class": r.recovery_class,
472	                        "proposed_by": r.recovery_class_proposed_by,
473	                        # The advisor's secondary note: a SOFT_STUB near the length ceiling is a
474	                        # length-decided boundary; flag it so the 2nd family confirms it, not a bare cut.
475	                        "boundary_for_adjudication": r.boundary_for_adjudication(),
476	                        "reference_trustworthy": r.reference_trustworthy(),
477	                        "reference_body_head": r.reference_body[:280],
478	                        "adjudicator_recovery_class": None,  # filled by the 2nd family / operator
479	                        "adjudicator_note": "",
480	                    },
481	                    ensure_ascii=False,
482	                )
483	                + "\n"
484	            )
485	
486	
487	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
488	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Build the fetch_crawl labeled ground-truth fixture.")
489	    parser.add_argument("--out", default=_DEFAULT_OUT, help="output fixture JSONL path")
490	    parser.add_argument(
491	        "--snapshot-root",
492	        action="append",
493	        default=None,
494	        help="corpus-snapshot root (repeatable); defaults to the 2 banked roots under POLARIS root",
495	    )
496	    parser.add_argument(
497	        "--emit-adjudication",
498	        default=None,
499	        help="optional path to write the two-family adjudication side file (PROPOSE-only hook)",
500	    )
501	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
502	
503	    rows = build_rows(args.snapshot_root)
504	    write_fixture(rows, args.out)
505	    summary = _stratification_summary(rows)
506	    print(f"[build_fixture] wrote {summary['total']} rows -> {args.out}")
507	    print(f"[build_fixture] by_source_type={summary['by_source_type']}")
508	    print(f"[build_fixture] by_recovery_class={summary['by_recovery_class']}")
509	    print(f"[build_fixture] trustworthy_reference_rows={summary['trustworthy_reference_rows']} "
510	          f"(gold RECOVERED — the rows where reference-recall is meaningful)")
511	    print(f"[build_fixture] boundary_rows_needing_adjudication={summary['boundary_rows_needing_adjudication']} "
512	          f"(SOFT_STUB near the length ceiling — route to --emit-adjudication for judge confirmation)")
513	    # Honest skew / target-band surfacing (advisor's note): never hide an uneven build.
514	    if summary["over_target_band"]:
515	        print(f"[build_fixture] NOTE: total {summary['total']} > target_max {summary['target_band'][1]} "
516	              f"(per-bucket caps overshoot slightly; honest artifact of stratification, not hidden).")
517	    if summary["under_target_band"]:
518	        print(f"[build_fixture] NOTE: total {summary['total']} < target_min {summary['target_band'][0]}.")
519	    if summary["source_type_max_bucket"] > 0 and summary["source_type_min_bucket"] > 0:
520	        print(f"[build_fixture] NOTE: source-type skew min_bucket={summary['source_type_min_bucket']} "
521	              f"max_bucket={summary['source_type_max_bucket']} (honest artifact of the banked data; "
522	              f"the metric pairs within-source-type so skew does not bias the winner).")
523	    if args.emit_adjudication:
524	        _emit_adjudication(rows, args.emit_adjudication)
525	        print(f"[build_fixture] adjudication side file -> {args.emit_adjudication}")
526	    return 0
527	
528	
529	if __name__ == "__main__":
530	    raise SystemExit(main())

content.text: All clean. Now the last file: fetch_crawl/gate0.py.

content.content:
1	"""I-ret-002 (#1294) layer 2 (fetch_crawl) — GATE-0 validity harness (the anti-drb_72 gate).
2	
3	NO candidate score in this layer is trusted until BOTH canaries below are green:
4	
5	A) SCORER-MATH CANARY (two-extreme, known input -> known score)
6	   * A "good" engine body == the gold reference body must score: recovery verdict == gold
7	     RECOVERED, reference_recall ~= 1.0, recovery_correct == True.
8	   * A "junk" engine body (a CAPTCHA / Cloudflare shell, an empty string) must score: verdict
9	     WALLED / FETCH_FAIL (NOT RECOVERED), reference_recall ~= 0.0, recovery_correct == False vs a
10	     RECOVERED gold row. If the scorer cannot separate these two extremes, the scorer math is
11	     broken and EVERY downstream number is meaningless -> FAIL LOUD (non-zero exit).
12	
13	B) PER-ENGINE LIVENESS CANARY (the drb_72 anti-pattern — the HIGHEST-priority check)
14	   A candidate that returns a stub / empty / load-fail / MISSING-KEY result must FAIL LOUD here,
15	   never silently score a believable-low number. Concretely:
16	     * zyte:       assert ZYTE_API_KEY is present AND a PG_ZYTE* knob is configured AND a known
17	                   fetchable URL returns a real (non-shell, non-empty) body. A keyless Zyte FAILS
18	                   LOUD (it is NOT allowed to "score low" — a keyless engine that scores 0.1 looks
19	                   like a bad engine when it is actually a dead one).
20	     * firecrawl:  assert FIRECRAWL_API_KEY present + a known URL returns a real body, else FAIL LOUD.
21	     * crawl4ai / playwright: assert the package imports AND a Chromium runtime is present AND a
22	                   known URL returns a real body, else FAIL LOUD.
23	   A "stub candidate" (an engine adapter that returns "" / a shell for everything) must be CAUGHT —
24	   that is the explicit liveness contract the smoke test exercises offline.
25	
26	MODES:
27	  * ``--scorer-only``  : run only the scorer-math canary (offline, no engines). Always available.
28	  * default (live)     : scorer canary + per-engine liveness on the REAL engines (needs keys/VM).
29	The offline smoke test calls ``run_scorer_canary`` and ``assert_engine_live`` with MOCKED engines.
30	"""
31	
32	from __future__ import annotations
33	
34	import argparse
35	import os
36	import sys
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from typing import Any, Callable
39	
40	from _polaris_root import ensure_on_syspath
41	
42	ensure_on_syspath()
43	
44	from build_fixture import (  # noqa: E402
45	    FETCH_FAIL,
46	    RECOVERED,
47	    WALLED,
48	    classify_recovery,
49	)
50	from run_bakeoff import (  # noqa: E402
51	    STATUS_NO_KEY,
52	    STATUS_SCORED,
53	    EngineSpec,
54	    engine_availability,
55	    engine_specs,
56	    reference_recall,
57	    score_url,
58	)
59	
60	
61	class GateZeroError(RuntimeError):
62	    """Raised FAIL-LOUD when a GATE-0 canary fails. A run that raises this is INVALID."""
63	
64	
65	# A known-fetchable canary URL for the liveness probe (a stable, robots-friendly endpoint that a
66	# healthy fetch engine returns a real body for). LAW VI env-overridable.
67	_ENV_LIVENESS_URL = "PG_FETCH_LIVENESS_URL"
68	_DEFAULT_LIVENESS_URL = "https://example.com/"
69	
70	# Minimum reference-recall the scorer canary's GOOD case must reach (it is the gold body itself, so
71	# this is ~1.0; we assert well above the junk case). The JUNK case must be at/below the junk ceiling.
72	_GOOD_RECALL_MIN = 0.95
73	_JUNK_RECALL_MAX = 0.05
74	
75	# A representative real CAPTCHA/Cloudflare shell body (the exact run-#7 anchor vocabulary) — must
76	# classify as WALLED and recall ~0 of a real gold body. Kept verbatim-ish so it exercises the SAME
77	# shell_detector path production uses.
78	_JUNK_SHELL_BODY = (
79	    "Just a moment... This page is displayed while the website verifies you are not a bot. "
80	    "Please complete the captcha challenge. Checking your browser before accessing. "
81	    "Cloudflare Ray ID. Performance & Security by Cloudflare."
82	)
83	
84	# A representative real main-content gold body (substantive clinical prose) for the GOOD case.
85	_GOOD_GOLD_BODY = (
86	    "Deep brain stimulation of the subthalamic nucleus significantly reduced motor symptoms in "
87	    "patients with advanced Parkinson disease compared with best medical therapy. In this "
88	    "randomized trial, the stimulation group showed a mean improvement of 41 percent on the "
89	    "unified Parkinson disease rating scale at six months, with reductions in dyskinesia and "
90	    "levodopa-equivalent daily dose. Adverse events included transient confusion and dysarthria."
91	)
92	
93	
94	# ── A) Scorer-math canary ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
95	
96	@dataclass
97	class CanaryResult:
98	    name: str
99	    passed: bool
100	    detail: dict[str, Any]
101	
102	
103	def _gold_tokens(body: str) -> set[str]:
104	    from build_fixture import content_tokens
105	
106	    return set(content_tokens(body))
107	
108	
109	def run_scorer_canary() -> list[CanaryResult]:
110	    """Two-extreme scorer-math canary. Returns the per-case results; raises if any FAILS LOUD."""
111	    results: list[CanaryResult] = []
112	    gold_tokens = _gold_tokens(_GOOD_GOLD_BODY)
113	
114	    # CASE 1: good body == gold on a TRUSTWORTHY (gold RECOVERED) row -> RECOVERED, recall ~1.0,
115	    # recovered True. This is a true main-content recovery.
116	    good = score_url(_GOOD_GOLD_BODY, RECOVERED, gold_tokens, reference_trustworthy=True)
117	    good_ok = (
118	        good["effective_recovery_class"] == RECOVERED
119	        and good["reference_recall"] >= _GOOD_RECALL_MIN
120	        and good["recovered"] is True
121	    )
122	    results.append(CanaryResult("scorer_good_body_recovered", good_ok, good))
123	    if not good_ok:
124	        raise GateZeroError(
125	            f"GATE-0 scorer canary FAILED (good case): a body identical to the gold reference on a "
126	            f"trustworthy row must score RECOVERED with recall>={_GOOD_RECALL_MIN} and recovered==True. "
127	            f"Got {good}. The scorer math is broken — every downstream number is meaningless."
128	        )
129	
130	    # CASE 2: junk shell body -> WALLED (a shell, NOT RECOVERED), recall ~0, recovered False. A
131	    # fetch-shell must NEVER count as a recovery (the drb_72 self-citation hole).
132	    junk = score_url(_JUNK_SHELL_BODY, RECOVERED, gold_tokens, reference_trustworthy=True)
133	    junk_ok = (
134	        junk["effective_recovery_class"] != RECOVERED
135	        and junk["reference_recall"] <= _JUNK_RECALL_MAX
136	        and junk["recovered"] is False
137	    )
138	    results.append(CanaryResult("scorer_junk_shell_not_recovered", junk_ok, junk))
139	    if not junk_ok:
140	        raise GateZeroError(
141	            f"GATE-0 scorer canary FAILED (junk case): a CAPTCHA/Cloudflare shell must NOT score "
142	            f"RECOVERED and must recall<={_JUNK_RECALL_MAX} of the gold body. Got {junk}. The "
143	            f"scorer would reward a fetch-shell as a recovery — the drb_72 self-citation hole."
144	        )
145	
146	    # CASE 3: empty body -> FETCH_FAIL, recall 0, recovered False.
147	    empty = score_url("", RECOVERED, gold_tokens, reference_trustworthy=True)
148	    empty_ok = (
149	        empty["effective_recovery_class"] == FETCH_FAIL
150	        and empty["reference_recall"] == 0.0
151	        and empty["recovered"] is False
152	    )
153	    results.append(CanaryResult("scorer_empty_body_fetch_fail", empty_ok, empty))
154	    if not empty_ok:
155	        raise GateZeroError(
156	            f"GATE-0 scorer canary FAILED (empty case): an empty body must score FETCH_FAIL with "
157	            f"recall 0.0 and recovered False. Got {empty}."
158	        )
159	
160	    # CASE 5 (the §-1.3 anti-inversion canary): an engine that BEATS a wall — returns a full real
161	    # article on a row the INCUMBENT walled (gold WALLED, NO trustworthy reference) — must be a WIN
162	    # (recovered True AND wall_broken True), NEVER scored wrong. This is the crown-jewel discriminator
163	    # (Zyte beating a paywall) and the exact inversion this metric was rebuilt to kill.
164	    wall_break = score_url(_GOOD_GOLD_BODY, WALLED, _gold_tokens(_JUNK_SHELL_BODY),
165	                           reference_trustworthy=False)
166	    wall_break_ok = (
167	        wall_break["effective_recovery_class"] == RECOVERED
168	        and wall_break["recovered"] is True
169	        and wall_break["wall_broken"] is True
170	        and wall_break["reference_meaningful"] is False
171	    )
172	    results.append(CanaryResult("scorer_wall_break_is_a_win_not_wrong", wall_break_ok, wall_break))
173	    if not wall_break_ok:
174	        raise GateZeroError(
175	            "GATE-0 scorer canary FAILED (wall-break case): an engine that recovers a full article "
176	            "on an incumbent-WALLED row (no trustworthy reference) MUST be a WIN (recovered + "
177	            f"wall_broken True), never scored wrong against the shell gold. Got {wall_break}. "
178	            "Scoring this as wrong is the §-1.3 inversion that punishes a valid better source."
179	        )
180	
181	    # CASE 4: classify_recovery direction sanity (the rubric the fixture trusts).
182	    direction_ok = (
183	        classify_recovery(_GOOD_GOLD_BODY) == RECOVERED
184	        and classify_recovery(_JUNK_SHELL_BODY) == WALLED
185	        and classify_recovery("") == FETCH_FAIL
186	    )
187	    results.append(CanaryResult("rubric_direction", direction_ok, {
188	        "good": classify_recovery(_GOOD_GOLD_BODY),
189	        "junk": classify_recovery(_JUNK_SHELL_BODY),
190	        "empty": classify_recovery(""),
191	    }))
192	    if not direction_ok:
193	        raise GateZeroError(
194	            "GATE-0 scorer canary FAILED (rubric direction): classify_recovery must map a real body "
195	            "-> RECOVERED, a CAPTCHA shell -> WALLED, an empty body -> FETCH_FAIL."
196	        )
197	    return results
198	
199	
200	# ── B) Per-engine liveness canary (the highest-priority drb_72 anti-pattern check) ──────
201	
202	def _zyte_knobs_present() -> bool:
203	    """True iff at least one PG_ZYTE* knob is configured (the brief's explicit Zyte liveness clause).
204	
205	    Zyte being keyless OR un-configured is a dead engine, not a low-quality one — the run is invalid
206	    until configured. We require the API key (asserted separately) AND a PG_ZYTE* knob so a half-wired
207	    Zyte (key but no enablement flag) is also caught.
208	    """
209	    return any(k.upper().startswith("PG_ZYTE") for k in os.environ)
210	
211	
212	def assert_engine_live(
213	    spec: EngineSpec,
214	    fetch_override: Callable[[str], str] | None = None,
215	    liveness_url: str | None = None,
216	) -> CanaryResult:
217	    """Per-engine liveness: a stub/empty/keyless/load-fail engine FAILS LOUD (never scores low).
218	
219	    ``fetch_override`` lets the smoke test inject a mocked engine (a real one OR a deliberately
220	    dead stub) with NO network. For the real VM run, ``fetch_override`` is None and the engine's
221	    production adapter is used.
222	
223	    Contract:
224	      * A no_key engine (e.g. keyless zyte) -> raise GateZeroError (NOT a low score).
225	      * zyte additionally requires a PG_ZYTE* knob configured.
226	      * A load_fail / needs_browser engine -> raise GateZeroError.
227	      * A SCORED engine whose canary fetch returns an empty / shell body -> raise GateZeroError
228	        (the engine is wired but DEAD — exactly the silent no-op the gate exists to catch).
229	      * Only a SCORED engine that returns a real, non-shell, content-bearing body PASSES.
230	    """
231	    url = liveness_url or os.environ.get(_ENV_LIVENESS_URL, _DEFAULT_LIVENESS_URL)
232	
233	    # Availability first (keys / package / browser) — unless the smoke injects a fetch (then the
234	    # smoke is asserting the fetch-result contract, having simulated availability).
235	    if fetch_override is None:
236	        status = engine_availability(spec)
237	        if spec.name == "zyte" and status == STATUS_SCORED and not _zyte_knobs_present():
238	            raise GateZeroError(
239	                "GATE-0 liveness FAILED (zyte): ZYTE_API_KEY is set but NO PG_ZYTE* knob is "
240	                "configured — Zyte is half-wired (a silent no-op without the enablement flag). "
241	                "Configure PG_ZYTE* on the VM. A keyless/half-wired Zyte must FAIL LOUD, never "
242	                "score low (the drb_72 anti-pattern)."
243	            )
244	        if status != STATUS_SCORED:
245	            raise GateZeroError(
246	                f"GATE-0 liveness FAILED ({spec.name}): availability={status} — a "
247	                f"{status} engine must FAIL LOUD, never be scored as a believable-low candidate. "
248	                f"{_status_remedy(spec, status)}"
249	            )
250	        fetch = _live_adapter(spec.name)
251	    else:
252	        fetch = fetch_override
253	
254	    try:
255	        body = fetch(url)
256	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a probe exception is a DEAD engine, fail loud.
257	        raise GateZeroError(
258	            f"GATE-0 liveness FAILED ({spec.name}): the canary fetch of {url} raised "
259	            f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc} — engine is wired but dead."
260	        ) from exc
261	
262	    cls = classify_recovery(body)
263	    recall_proxy = reference_recall(body, _gold_tokens(body))  # self-recall is 1.0 for non-empty
264	    if not (body or "").strip():
265	        raise GateZeroError(
266	            f"GATE-0 liveness FAILED ({spec.name}): the canary fetch of {url} returned an EMPTY "
267	            f"body. A stub/dead engine must fail loud here, never score a believable-low number."
268	        )
269	    if cls in (WALLED, FETCH_FAIL):
270	        raise GateZeroError(
271	            f"GATE-0 liveness FAILED ({spec.name}): the canary fetch of {url} returned a "
272	            f"{cls} body (a shell / dead fetch), not real content. Engine is not live."
273	        )
274	    return CanaryResult(
275	        f"liveness_{spec.name}", True,
276	        {"url": url, "recovery_class": cls, "self_recall": round(recall_proxy, 3),
277	         "body_len": len((body or '').strip())},
278	    )
279	
280	
281	def _status_remedy(spec: EngineSpec, status: str) -> str:
282	    if status == STATUS_NO_KEY:
283	        return f"Set {spec.needs_key_env} on the VM."
284	    return {
285	        "load_fail": f"Install {spec.pip_id}.",
286	        "needs_browser": "Run `playwright install chromium`.",
287	    }.get(status, "")
288	
289	
290	def _live_adapter(name: str) -> Callable[[str], str]:
291	    from run_bakeoff import _FETCH_ADAPTERS
292	
293	    return _FETCH_ADAPTERS[name]
294	
295	
296	# ── Driver ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
297	
298	def run_gate0(scorer_only: bool = False, engines_to_probe: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
299	    """Run GATE-0. Returns 0 iff all canaries pass; raises GateZeroError (caught -> exit 2) on fail."""
300	    print("[gate0] A) scorer-math canary ...")
301	    for r in run_scorer_canary():
302	        print(f"  [{'PASS' if r.passed else 'FAIL'}] {r.name}: {r.detail}")
303	    if scorer_only:
304	        print("[gate0] scorer-only mode: skipping live per-engine liveness (run on the VM with keys).")
305	        return 0
306	
307	    print("[gate0] B) per-engine liveness canary (FAIL LOUD on stub/keyless/dead) ...")
308	    specs = engine_specs()
309	    if engines_to_probe:
310	        specs = [s for s in specs if s.name in set(engines_to_probe)]
311	    for spec in specs:
312	        r = assert_engine_live(spec)
313	        print(f"  [PASS] {r.name}: {r.detail}")
314	    return 0
315	
316	
317	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
318	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="fetch_crawl GATE-0 validity harness.")
319	    parser.add_argument("--scorer-only", action="store_true",
320	                        help="run only the offline scorer-math canary (no engines)")
321	    parser.add_argument("--engine", action="append", default=None,
322	                        help="probe only these engines for liveness (repeatable)")
323	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
324	    try:
325	        return run_gate0(scorer_only=args.scorer_only, engines_to_probe=args.engine)
326	    except GateZeroError as exc:
327	        print(f"[gate0] FAIL LOUD: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
328	        return 2
329	
330	
331	if __name__ == "__main__":
332	    raise SystemExit(main())
333
